input.command:
cd /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ
gh issue create --title "comparison:columns mode — port FDC's column-level diff (+ optimization pass over both grains)" --label enhancement --milestone "v1.1 Backlog" --body "User follow-up to the ADR 0015 build (#791–#795). FDC has two grains: record-level (\`record_comparison.py\`, being ported in #793 as \`comparison:records\`) and column-level (\`column_comparison.py\`, reserved as \`comparison:columns\`).

**Scope**
- Port the column-level diff with the same discipline as #793: pure module, FDC bucket semantics (per-column matched/mismatch/additional dicts), DataQ tooling, adversarial battery.
- Optimization pass over BOTH grains beyond FDC's whole-frame \`pd.merge\`: vectorized key-hash compare, batched/chunked merges via the DatasetReader, key-only first pass to size buckets before materializing values, and (for SQL↔SQL same-engine pairs) evaluate pushdown count/hash pre-checks. FDC as-written is fine for a local tool but loads both frames whole and does per-column Python loops — the port should not inherit that ceiling.
- Wire \`comparison:columns\` into the ADR 0015 authoring seam (canonical type already reserved) + editor mode toggle.

**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] comparison:columns authorable + runnable with FDC-parity buckets
- [ ] Benchmark note vs the naive port (1M-row fixture) recorded in the PR
- [ ] Both grains share the DatasetReader cap/fail-fast semantics" 2>&1 | tail -1
gh issue create --title "Verify + document Astronomer (Astro) support for the Airflow OrchestrationProvider" --label enhancement --label documentation --milestone "v1.1 Backlog" --body "User question: does the Airflow orchestration handle Astronomer jobs?

**In principle yes** — Astro runs standard Airflow, and both our channels bind to vanilla surfaces (ADR 0007): the HMAC webhook callback is user code inside the DAG (works on any deployment that can reach DataQ), and the 10-min polling fallback uses the stock Airflow REST API (\`/api/v1/dags/.../dagRuns\`). The open questions are Astro-specific auth + reachability:
- Astro's REST API access uses **Astro API tokens / Deployment API tokens** (Bearer) rather than basic auth — confirm the Airflow connection adapter's auth modes cover it (or add a token mode).
- The deployment URL shape (\`https://<deployment>.astronomer.run/<suffix>/api/v1\`) — confirm base-URL config handles it.
- Egress: the callback needs the Astro deployment to reach the DataQ webhook URL.

**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Adapter auth verified/extended for Astro API tokens
- [ ] docs (feature-matrix + user docs) state Astro support + setup steps
- [ ] Smoke either against a free Astro trial deployment or documented as untested-but-compatible" 2>&1 | tail -1
gh issue create --title "Mobile: sidebar/hamburger menu should overlay the page, not consume layout width" --label enhancement --milestone "v1.1 Backlog" --body "User-reported (follow-up to the #617/#688 responsive pass): on mobile, expanding the hamburger/Sider squeezes the page content because the Sider participates in the flex layout. It should render as an **overlay drawer** (AntD \`Drawer\` or Sider with \`breakpoint\` + fixed positioning + scrim) above the content at small viewports; desktop behaviour unchanged.

**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] ≤ md viewports: expanded nav overlays content (scrim behind, content width unchanged)
- [ ] Desktop unchanged
- [ ] Playwright/component test pins the overlay behaviour at 390px" 2>&1 | tail -1
gh issue create --title "Assets: hierarchical browse under Connections (connection → database → schema → table), not a flat table" --label enhancement --milestone "v1.1 Backlog" --body "User-reported (follow-up to the G-d asset views #760/#773): the current Assets page is a single flat table with one row per object. Wanted instead:

1. Assets grouped **under their connection** (as a separate view, not inside the existing connection tab).
2. **Drill-down hierarchy**: connections list → click a connection → its databases/catalogs → click → schemas/namespaces → click → tables/paths (the asset leaves). Asset identity stays the ADR 0034 OpenLineage namespace+name — this is a presentation regrouping, the namespace already encodes the connection/db/schema segments.

**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Tree/drill-down browse rooted at connections; leaves open the existing AssetDetail
- [ ] Env grouping preserved (DEV/QA distinct assets, grouped visually per ADR 0034)
- [ ] Flat table retained as a secondary 'all assets' list/search view (or explicitly dropped with user sign-off)" 2>&1 | tail -1
gh issue create --title "AssetDetail: split Health into connection health vs suite-results health" --label enhancement --milestone "v1.1 Backlog" --body "User-reported: the asset Health signal conflates two different things. Split into:
- **Asset connection health** — can DataQ currently reach the datasource backing this asset (connection test status / last successful run against it / operational errors).
- **Suite health** — the ADR 0005 severity-weighted score of the suites attached to this asset (the existing signal).

**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Two clearly-labelled health indicators on AssetDetail (and the assets list lead strip where applicable)
- [ ] Connection-health derivation documented (no new polling loop without design note)
- [ ] Operational \`error\`/\`skip\` results feed connection health, not suite health (matches #122 semantics)" 2>&1 | tail -1
gh issue create --title "Harness: stand up Marquez + backfill lineage so prod assets show lineage" --label ops --milestone "v1.1 Backlog" --body "User-reported: in prod none of the assets have lineage details. The #762 \`LineageProvider\` seam + Marquez reference impl and the dev-only compose profile exist, but no Marquez runs against the deployed app and the OL emitter is dark-by-default (ADR 0034), so \`lineage_edges\` only fills from dbt manifests (#759) where configured.

**Scope (harness-side, ADR 0021 — tracked here since the harness repo is untracked)**
- Add a Marquez service to the harness (or a small ACA app while the Azure window lasts; local compose after wind-down).
- Enable OL emission (+ dbt lineage refresh) for the harness flows so Flow A/B/C/D assets get real upstream/downstream edges.
- Verify AssetDetail lineage renders end-to-end against it.

**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Marquez reachable from the app (env-configured, off by default)
- [ ] At least the Flow-A chain shows ≥2 lineage edges in the UI
- [ ] Runbook section in the harness README / wake-window script flag" 2>&1 | tail -1
gh issue create --title "Lineage: graph view (upstream LHS → asset → downstream RHS) instead of separate boxes" --label enhancement --milestone "v1.1 Backlog" --body "User-reported (follow-up to #760/#761): AssetDetail renders upstream and downstream as two separate list boxes. Replace with a single **left-to-right graph** — upstream nodes on the left, the current asset centered, downstream on the right; nodes clickable (navigate to that asset), depth ≥2 via the existing blast-radius BFS.

Implementation note: prefer a light dependency (e.g. reactflow) — check the ADR 0031 license guardrail (MIT ok) and lazy-load like recharts (ADR 0022).

**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Graph view with directional layout + clickable nodes
- [ ] Handles 0-edge assets gracefully (current empty-state preserved)
- [ ] Mobile: horizontal scroll inside the card, no page overflow
- [ ] Dependency license checked (no strong-copyleft, CONTRIBUTING rule 40)" 2>&1 | tail -1
gh issue create --title "chore: stale '# nosec B105' on dashboard_service penalty mapping — bandit warns 'no failed test'" --label refactor --milestone "v1.1 Backlog" --body "Pre-existing hygiene found during the #791 gate run (filed per the fix-or-file rule): \`bandit -q -r backend/app -c pyproject.toml\` emits three warnings — \`nosec encountered (B105), but no failed test\` at \`backend/app/services/dashboard_service.py:35–37\`. The \`# nosec B105\` on the \`_PENALTY\` mapping is stale (current pinned bandit no longer flags those dict literals as hardcoded passwords), so the suppression is dead and noisy.

**Acceptance criteria**
- [ ] Remove the stale \`# nosec B105\` (or re-scope it) — bandit run stays clean with zero warnings
- [ ] Verified against the CI-pinned bandit version" 2>&1 | tail -1

content.text:
While #798's CI runs, here's where everything stands plus the answers you asked for:

## Status

- **ADR 0015 merged** ([#790](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/790)) — suite = target under test; comparison check adds one source ref. Your direction question is settled in the merged text: **LHS = source (user-picked baseline), RHS = target (locked to the suite's connection)**.
- **#791 (schema + authoring slice) is built and in PR [#798](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/798)**, auto-merge armed: migration `b7c8d9e0f1a2` (up+down tested), full authoring validation (source spec via the target resolver, per-side read-only SQL, join keys, cross-env allowed, orchestration sources rejected), RESTRICT delete guard with a 409 naming dependents, export/import `(name, env)` round-trip, and a run-path guard so a comparison check yields a per-check `error` result (not a failed run) until #794 lands. 2015 tests green, 98.28% coverage. The agentic review found five real issues (TOCTOU 500 on the delete race, `max_rows: true` passing as 1, comparison configs bypassing the #651 size cap, unbounded-looking truncated 409 list, MCP tool unable to author comparisons) — **all five fixed in-PR** and posted/resolved on the PR.
- **En route, a real regression fixed**: [#796](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/796) — the #783 rate limiter was 429-ing CI's Playwright lane (tokenless dev-bypass = one per-IP bucket); fixed in merged [#797](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/797) and recorded in memory.
- **Your 8 follow-ups filed** as [#799](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/799)–[#805](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/805) (columns-mode + optimization, Astronomer verification, mobile overlay nav, hierarchical asset browse — I merged your bullets 4+5 into one issue, health split, Marquez in harness, lineage graph view) plus [#806](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/806) (a stale `# nosec` I found during the gates, filed per your fix-or-file rule).

## Your two questions

**Can the FDC comparison logic be optimized?** Yes, and it should be. FDC as-written does whole-frame `pd.merge`s and per-column Python loops with everything in memory — right for a local tool, a ceiling for a shared platform. The port already has explicit ADR-0015 latitude: batched/chunked reads through the `DatasetReader`, key-hash first pass before materializing values, and pushdown count/hash pre-checks for SQL↔SQL pairs. Record grain gets this in #793; column grain + a benchmarked optimization pass over both is #799.

**Does Airflow orchestration handle Astronomer?** In principle yes — Astro runs vanilla Airflow and both our channels bind to standard surfaces (ADR 0007): the HMAC callback is DAG-side user code, and polling uses the stock `dagRuns` REST API. The genuinely open bits are Astro's **API-token auth** (vs basic auth — our adapter may need a bearer mode) and its deployment URL shape. That verification is #800.

I'll continue with slice 2 (#792, the `DatasetReader` seam) once #798 merges.

def infer_file_format(path: str, explicit: str | None) -> str:
    """Resolve the file format from an explicit value or the path extension.

    Raises `ProfileTargetInvalidError` (422) for an unknown/unsupported format —
    the caller can always pass `file_format` to override extension guessing. The
    extension mapping is shared with the runner (`flatfile.format_from_path`).
    """
    fmt = explicit or format_from_path(path)
    if fmt not in _SUPPORTED_FORMATS:
        raise ProfileTargetInvalidError(
            "cannot determine a supported file format; pass file_format",
            detail={"path": path, "supported": sorted(_SUPPORTED_FORMATS)},
        )
    return fmt


def _to_native(value: Any) -> Any:
    """Coerce a numpy/pandas scalar to a JSON-friendly Python value."""
    if value is None:
        return None
    if hasattr(value, "isoformat"):  # Timestamp / datetime / date
        return value.isoformat()
    if hasattr(value, "item"):  # numpy scalar → Python scalar
        value = value.item()
    if isinstance(value, float) and math.isnan(value):
        return None
    if isinstance(value, bool | int | float | str):
        return value
    # Anything else a column can hold (bytes/binary, Decimal, UUID, …) → a display
    # string, so a min/max/top value is always JSON-encodable, never a 500 at the
    # response boundary. `bool` is matched above `int` since bool is an int.
    return str(value)


def _profile_columns(df: Any, *, columns: list[str], top_n: int) -> tuple[int, list[ColumnProfile]]:
    """Row count + per-column stats for `columns` of an in-memory dataframe.

    The datasource-neutral core of the pandas profiling path, shared by the
    flat-file (`profile_dataframe`) and Iceberg (`profile_iceberg`) profilers so
    they can't drift on the stats contract — only the `ProfileResult` identity
    fields (`path`/`file_format` vs `table`) differ per datasource. Raises
    `ProfileColumnNotFoundError` (422) if a requested column isn't in the frame —
    a clean error instead of a KeyError 500.
    """
    missing = [c for c in columns if c not in df.columns]
    if missing:
        raise ProfileColumnNotFoundError(
            "requested column(s) not in the target",
            detail={"missing": missing, "available": [str(c) for c in df.columns][:50]},
        )
    row_count = len(df)
    profiles = [_profile_series(col, df[col], row_count=row_count, top_n=top_n) for col in columns]
    return row_count, profiles


def profile_dataframe(
    df: Any, *, columns: list[str], top_n: int, path: str, file_format: str
) -> ProfileResult:
    """Compute per-column stats from an in-memory dataframe (pure, no I/O).

    Raises `ProfileColumnNotFoundError` (422) if a requested column isn't in the
    frame — a clean error instead of a KeyError 500.
    """
    row_count, profiles = _profile_columns(df, columns=columns, top_n=top_n)
    return ProfileResult(path=path, file_format=file_format, row_count=row_count, columns=profiles)

def validate_identifier(name: str | None) -> str:
    """Validate `name` against the plain-identifier allowlist and return it.

    Raises `ProfileIdentifierInvalidError` (422) for anything that isn't a plain
    identifier. The SQLAlchemy Core builders quote safely on their own; this is
    defence-in-depth and turns an odd name into a clean 422 instead of a quoted
    column that simply doesn't exist.
    """
    if not name or not _IDENTIFIER.match(name):
        raise ProfileIdentifierInvalidError(
            "not a valid table/schema/column identifier", detail={"identifier": name}
        )
    return name


def _table(schema: str, table_name: str, catalog: str | None = None) -> Any:
    """A Core table clause, optionally with a 3-level namespace (Unity Catalog).

    With a `catalog`, the namespace is `catalog.schema` passed as an unquoted
    `quoted_name` so the dialect emits three dotted parts (`catalog.schema.table`)
    rather than quoting the dotted string as one identifier. Safe because every
    part is allowlist-validated.
    """
    validate_identifier(schema)
    if catalog is not None:
        validate_identifier(catalog)
        namespace: Any = quoted_name(f"{catalog}.{schema}", quote=False)
    else:
        namespace = schema
    return table(validate_identifier(table_name), schema=namespace)

        namespace: Any = quoted_name(f"{catalog}.{schema}", quote=False)
    else:
        namespace = schema
    return table(validate_identifier(table_name), schema=namespace)


def build_aggregate_query(
    schema: str, table_name: str, columns: list[str], catalog: str | None = None

input.content:
"""Read one comparison side (source or target) into a DataFrame (ADR 0015, #792).

The `DatasetReader` seam: given a **datasource** connection and a `DatasetSpec`
(a resolved table triple, a flat-file path, or a read-only SQL projection),
return a bounded pandas DataFrame for the comparison engine (#793). Grown from
plumbing that already exists — the profiler's SQL engine access
(`profile_service`), `flatfile`'s object read, `pyiceberg`'s scan — behind the
same dict-dispatch shape as the profiler's `_PROFILERS`, so service code never
branches on `connection.type`. Orchestration types have no reader (a clean 422,
mirroring `build_check_runner`).

Row-cap discipline (ADR 0015 §3): `max_rows` is enforced **fail-fast** — a
cheap COUNT preflight where the backend offers one (SQL engines, Iceberg scan
metadata) so an oversized table never transfers, plus a post-read length check
everywhere (the preflight is racy by nature; flat files have no preflight at
all). Over-cap raises `DatasetTooLargeError`, **never** a silent truncation —
a truncated diff produces confidently wrong mismatch buckets, which is worse
than no answer.

FastAPI-free like the sibling services: takes ORM `Connection` + `SecretStore`,
returns DataFrames, raises `DataQError` subclasses (the #794 run path maps them
to operational `error` results; the authoring/dry-run paths surface them as
422s).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import Callable
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Any

import sqlalchemy as sa

from backend.app.core.config import get_settings
from backend.app.core.errors import DataQError
from backend.app.core.logging import get_logger
from backend.app.core.secrets import SecretStore
from backend.app.datasources.flatfile import read_dataframe as read_flatfile_dataframe
from backend.app.datasources.iceberg import (
    IcebergConfig,
    load_iceberg_table,
    read_iceberg_dataframe,
)
from backend.app.db.models import Connection
from backend.app.services.custom_sql import validate_query
from backend.app.services.profile_service import (
    _open_connection,
    _table,
    resolve_effective_schema,
)

log = get_logger(__name__)


class DatasetReadUnsupportedError(DataQError):
    status_code = 422
    code = "comparison_read_unsupported"


class DatasetTooLargeError(DataQError):
    status_code = 422
    code = "comparison_dataset_too_large"


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class DatasetSpec:
    """What to read from one comparison side.

    Exactly one addressing mode is used per datasource: `query` (SQL sources,
    already author-time validated read-only), the `table`/`schema`/`catalog`
    triple (SQL sources; `table` also carries the Iceberg `namespace.table`
    identifier), or `path` (flat-file sources — the caller materializes batch
    targets to a concrete path first, exactly like a run does).
    """

    table: str | None = None
    schema: str | None = None
    catalog: str | None = None
    path: str | None = None
    query: str | None = None


def default_max_rows() -> int:
    """The settings-level comparison row cap (per-check `config.max_rows`
    overrides it — resolved by the caller, ADR 0015 §3)."""
    return get_settings().comparison_max_rows


def _too_large(count: int, max_rows: int, *, side_hint: str) -> DatasetTooLargeError:
    return DatasetTooLargeError(
        f"{side_hint} has {count} rows, over the comparison cap of {max_rows} — "
        "raise config.max_rows deliberately or narrow the dataset (a truncated "
        "diff would be silently wrong, so DataQ refuses instead)",
        detail={"rows": count, "max_rows": max_rows},
    )


def _require_secret(connection: Connection, secret_store: SecretStore) -> str:
    if not connection.secret_ref:
        raise DatasetReadUnsupportedError(
            "connection has no stored credential",
            detail={"connection_id": str(connection.id)},
        )
    return secret_store.get(connection.secret_ref)


# ───────────────────────── SQL (snowflake / unity_catalog) ──────────


def _wrapped_query(spec: DatasetSpec) -> str:
    """The validated read-only projection as a parenthesized FROM source.

    Re-validated here (defence in depth — author-time validation already ran):
    a single read-only SELECT/WITH statement, so interpolating it into the
    COUNT/LIMIT wrappers below cannot smuggle a second statement or a write.
    """
    assert spec.query is not None
    validate_query(spec.query)
    return spec.query.rstrip().rstrip(";")


def _sql_read(
    connection: Connection, spec: DatasetSpec, max_rows: int, secret_store: SecretStore
) -> Any:
    import pandas as pd

    count_stmt: Any
    select_stmt: Any
    if spec.query is not None:
        q = _wrapped_query(spec)
        # Interpolation is safe: `q` passed the read-only single-statement
        # validator (ADR 0019) at author time AND immediately above.
        count_stmt = sa.text(f"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ({q}) __dataq_src")  # noqa: S608  # nosec B608
        select_stmt = sa.text(
            f"SELECT * FROM ({q}) __dataq_src LIMIT {int(max_rows) + 1}"  # noqa: S608  # nosec B608
        )
    else:
        schema = resolve_effective_schema(connection, spec.schema)
        source = _table(schema, spec.table or "", spec.catalog)
        count_stmt = sa.select(sa.func.count()).select_from(source)
        select_stmt = sa.select(sa.text("*")).select_from(source).limit(max_rows + 1)

    with _open_connection(connection, secret_store) as conn:
        count = int(conn.execute(count_stmt).scalar_one())
        if count > max_rows:
            raise _too_large(count, max_rows, side_hint="dataset")
        df = pd.read_sql(select_stmt, conn)
    # Belt over the braces: the COUNT is racy (rows can land between preflight
    # and read) and the LIMIT is max_rows+1 exactly so growth is detectable.
    if len(df) > max_rows:
        raise _too_large(len(df), max_rows, side_hint="dataset")
    return df


# ───────────────────────── flat file (adls_gen2 / s3) ───────────────


def _flatfile_read(
    connection: Connection, spec: DatasetSpec, max_rows: int, secret_store: SecretStore
) -> Any:
    path = spec.path or spec.table
    if not path:
        raise DatasetReadUnsupportedError(
            "flat-file comparison side needs a resolved path", detail={"spec": "path"}
        )
    secret = _require_secret(connection, secret_store)
    # No cheap preflight exists for an object read — the whole file downloads,
    # then the cap is enforced before any diffing.
    df = read_flatfile_dataframe(
        conn_type=connection.type, config=dict(connection.config), path=path, secret=secret
    )
    if len(df) > max_rows:
        raise _too_large(len(df), max_rows, side_hint=f"file {path!r}")
    return df


# ───────────────────────── iceberg (native pyiceberg) ───────────────


def _iceberg_read(
    connection: Connection, spec: DatasetSpec, max_rows: int, secret_store: SecretStore
) -> Any:
    if not spec.table:
        raise DatasetReadUnsupportedError(
            "iceberg comparison side needs a namespace.table identifier",
            detail={"spec": "table"},
        )
    cfg = IcebergConfig.model_validate(connection.config)
    # Credential optional, mirroring `build_iceberg_runner` (a local warehouse /
    # vended-credentials REST catalog has none).
    secret = secret_store.get(connection.secret_ref) if connection.secret_ref else None
    table = load_iceberg_table(cfg, secret, spec.table)
    count = int(table.scan().count())  # snapshot metadata — no data files read
    if count > max_rows:
        raise _too_large(count, max_rows, side_hint=f"iceberg table {spec.table!r}")
    df = read_iceberg_dataframe(cfg, secret, spec.table, limit=max_rows + 1, table=table)
    if len(df) > max_rows:
        raise _too_large(len(df), max_rows, side_hint=f"iceberg table {spec.table!r}")
    return df


# ───────────────────────── dispatch ─────────────────────────────────

_Reader = Callable[[Connection, DatasetSpec, int, SecretStore], Any]

_READERS: dict[str, _Reader] = {
    "snowflake": _sql_read,
    "unity_catalog": _sql_read,
    "adls_gen2": _flatfile_read,
    "s3": _flatfile_read,
    "iceberg": _iceberg_read,
}


def read_dataset(
    connection: Connection,
    spec: DatasetSpec,
    *,
    max_rows: int,
    secret_store: SecretStore,
) -> Any:
    """Read one comparison side as a pandas DataFrame, capped at `max_rows`.

    Raises `DatasetReadUnsupportedError` (no reader for the connection type or
    an unusable spec) or `DatasetTooLargeError` (over-cap, fail-fast).
    """
    reader = _READERS.get(connection.type)
    if reader is None:
        raise DatasetReadUnsupportedError(
            f"connection type {connection.type!r} has no dataset reader "
            "(orchestration providers are never comparison sides)",
            detail={"type": connection.type, "supported": sorted(_READERS)},
        )
    if max_rows <= 0:
        raise DatasetReadUnsupportedError(
            "max_rows must be positive", detail={"max_rows": max_rows}
        )
    df = reader(connection, spec, max_rows, secret_store)
    log.info(
        "comparison_side_read",
        connection_id=str(connection.id),
        connection_type=connection.type,
        rows=len(df),
        max_rows=max_rows,
    )
    return df

36	| `CORS_ALLOW_ORIGINS` | n/a (same-origin) | empty — the frontend Container App proxies `/api` same-origin (ADR 0028); set the SPA origin only if you split them. |
37	| `PUBLIC_BASE_URL` | n/a | the public origin — used to assemble inbound webhook URLs **and** the "View run" deep links in Slack/email alerts (#416); unset → alerts omit the link. |
38	| `WORKSPACE_ADMIN_EMAILS` | seeded dev user | a **minimal** real allowlist — admins can read every suite's failing-row samples (see [Operational notes](#operational-notes)). |
39	| `RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED` | `true` | keep **`true`** — the fixed-window throttle on every public surface (REST + webhooks + `/mcp`), ADR 0035. Fail-open (a Redis outage disables it, logged); set `false` only to fully disable. |
40	| `RATE_LIMIT_AUTHENTICATED_PER_MINUTE` / `RATE_LIMIT_UNAUTHENTICATED_PER_MINUTE` / `RATE_LIMIT_WEBHOOK_PER_MINUTE` | `300` / `120` / `120` | per-minute caps, keyed per `sha256(bearer)` (authenticated) or client-IP (rest). Defaults are generous; **tighten `RATE_LIMIT_WEBHOOK_PER_MINUTE`** to your orchestrator's real callback cadence. |
41	| `RATE_LIMIT_IP_PER_MINUTE` | `1200` | per-IP ceiling across **all** bearer buckets from one IP — the rotated-token backstop (a client cycling a fresh random `Bearer` per request can't mint unlimited fresh per-token buckets to dodge the cap). Applies only to the `default` (bearer) class; the webhook/unauth classes are already per-IP. |
42	| `RATE_LIMIT_XFF_TRUSTED_HOPS` | `1` | number of trusted proxies that append `X-Forwarded-For` in your deployment — the real client is the entry that many hops from the right. **`1`** for a single-proxy / compose setup (rightmost); **`3`** for the ACA public-envoy→nginx→internal-envoy chain (set in Terraform). A chain shorter than this falls back to the socket peer. |
43	| `APPLICATIONINSIGHTS_CONNECTION_STRING` | unset | Azure Monitor / App Insights backend for spans + logs (observability, OTel — ADR 0010). |
44	| `OTEL_EXPORTER_OTLP_ENDPOINT` | unset | generic OTLP/HTTP backend for spans + logs (#589) — any OTLP consumer (Tempo/Jaeger/Datadog/Collector); set alongside App Insights for parity, or alone for a non-Azure deploy. |
45	| `OPENLINEAGE_URL` | unset | OpenLineage emission (ADR 0034, #758) — **dark by default**: unset ⇒ zero emission. Point at an OL receiver (Marquez, DataHub's OL endpoint) and every suite run emits START + terminal RunEvents with DQ facets (5s emit timeout, fail-open, no sample rows ever). Advanced transports via the library-owned `OPENLINEAGE__TRANSPORT__*` / `OPENLINEAGE_CONFIG`; `OPENLINEAGE_DISABLED=true` forces dark. |

input.content:
"""DatasetReader seam tests (ADR 0015, #792) — no live datasources.

The live I/O seams (`_open_connection`, the flat-file/iceberg read functions)
are monkeypatched exactly like the profiler's tests; what's under test is the
dispatch, the fail-fast row-cap discipline (COUNT preflight + post-read race
guard, never truncation), the read-time read-only re-validation, and the
credential handling.
"""

import uuid
from contextlib import contextmanager
from typing import Any

import pandas as pd
import pytest

from backend.app.db.models import Connection
from backend.app.services import dataset_reader
from backend.app.services.custom_sql import CustomSqlInvalidError
from backend.app.services.dataset_reader import (
    DatasetReadUnsupportedError,
    DatasetSpec,
    DatasetTooLargeError,
    read_dataset,
)


class FakeSecretStore:
    def __init__(self, secret: str | None = "s3cret") -> None:
        self._secret = secret
        self.requested: list[str] = []

    def get(self, ref: str) -> str:
        self.requested.append(ref)
        assert self._secret is not None
        return self._secret


def _conn(conn_type: str, *, secret_ref: str | None = "conn-x", **config: Any) -> Connection:
    return Connection(
        id=uuid.uuid4(),
        name=f"{conn_type}-t",
        type=conn_type,
        env="dev",
        config=config,
        secret_ref=secret_ref,
        created_by=uuid.uuid4(),
    )


def _frame(rows: int) -> pd.DataFrame:
    return pd.DataFrame({"id": range(rows)})


class FakeSqlConnection:
    """Stands in for the SQLAlchemy connection `_open_connection` yields."""

    def __init__(self, count: int) -> None:
        self._count = count
        self.statements: list[str] = []

    def execute(self, stmt: Any) -> Any:
        self.statements.append(str(stmt))
        count = self._count

        class _Result:
            def scalar_one(self) -> int:
                return count

        return _Result()


def _patch_sql(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, *, count: int, frame: pd.DataFrame
) -> FakeSqlConnection:
    fake = FakeSqlConnection(count)

    @contextmanager
    def _fake_open(connection: Connection, secret_store: Any) -> Any:
        yield fake

    monkeypatch.setattr(dataset_reader, "_open_connection", _fake_open)
    monkeypatch.setattr(pd, "read_sql", lambda stmt, conn: frame)
    return fake


# ───────────────────────── dispatch ─────────────────────────────────


def test_orchestration_type_has_no_reader() -> None:
    with pytest.raises(DatasetReadUnsupportedError, match="orchestration"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("airflow"),
            DatasetSpec(table="t"),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_non_positive_cap_rejected() -> None:
    with pytest.raises(DatasetReadUnsupportedError, match="max_rows"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("snowflake"),
            DatasetSpec(table="t", schema="s"),
            max_rows=0,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_default_max_rows_reads_settings() -> None:
    assert dataset_reader.default_max_rows() == 100_000


# ───────────────────────── SQL path ─────────────────────────────────


def test_sql_table_read_happy_path(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    fake = _patch_sql(monkeypatch, count=3, frame=_frame(3))
    df = read_dataset(
        _conn("snowflake"),
        DatasetSpec(table="ORDERS", schema="RETAIL"),
        max_rows=10,
        secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )
    assert len(df) == 3
    # COUNT preflight ran before the read.
    assert "count" in fake.statements[0].lower()


def test_sql_count_preflight_fails_fast_without_reading(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    def _explode(stmt: Any, conn: Any) -> Any:
        raise AssertionError("read_sql must not run when the preflight is over-cap")

    fake = FakeSqlConnection(count=11)

    @contextmanager
    def _fake_open(connection: Connection, secret_store: Any) -> Any:
        yield fake

    monkeypatch.setattr(dataset_reader, "_open_connection", _fake_open)
    monkeypatch.setattr(pd, "read_sql", _explode)
    with pytest.raises(DatasetTooLargeError, match="11 rows"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("unity_catalog"),
            DatasetSpec(table="t", schema="s", catalog="c"),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_sql_post_read_race_guard(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    # COUNT said 10 (under cap) but rows landed before the read → the LIMIT
    # max_rows+1 read surfaces 11 rows → refuse, never diff a truncated frame.
    _patch_sql(monkeypatch, count=10, frame=_frame(11))
    with pytest.raises(DatasetTooLargeError):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("snowflake"),
            DatasetSpec(table="t", schema="s"),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_sql_query_spec_wraps_and_limits(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    fake = _patch_sql(monkeypatch, count=2, frame=_frame(2))
    df = read_dataset(
        _conn("snowflake"),
        DatasetSpec(query="SELECT id FROM RETAIL.ORDERS;"),
        max_rows=10,
        secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )
    assert len(df) == 2
    assert "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT id FROM RETAIL.ORDERS) __dataq_src" in fake.statements[0]


def test_sql_query_revalidated_read_only_at_read_time(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    # Defence in depth: even if a writeful query somehow reached storage, the
    # reader re-validates before interpolating it into the wrappers.
    _patch_sql(monkeypatch, count=1, frame=_frame(1))
    with pytest.raises(CustomSqlInvalidError):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("snowflake"),
            DatasetSpec(query="DELETE FROM ORDERS"),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


# ───────────────────────── flat-file path ───────────────────────────


def test_flatfile_read_and_cap(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        dataset_reader, "read_flatfile_dataframe", lambda **kw: _frame(5)
    )
    store = FakeSecretStore()
    df = read_dataset(
        _conn("s3", bucket="b"),
        DatasetSpec(path="orders.csv"),
        max_rows=5,
        secret_store=store,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )
    assert len(df) == 5 and store.requested == ["conn-x"]

    monkeypatch.setattr(
        dataset_reader, "read_flatfile_dataframe", lambda **kw: _frame(6)
    )
    with pytest.raises(DatasetTooLargeError, match="orders.csv"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("adls_gen2"),
            DatasetSpec(path="orders.csv"),
            max_rows=5,
            secret_store=store,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_flatfile_requires_secret_and_path() -> None:
    with pytest.raises(DatasetReadUnsupportedError, match="credential"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("s3", secret_ref=None),
            DatasetSpec(path="orders.csv"),
            max_rows=5,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )
    with pytest.raises(DatasetReadUnsupportedError, match="path"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("s3"),
            DatasetSpec(),
            max_rows=5,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


# ───────────────────────── iceberg path ─────────────────────────────


class FakeIcebergTable:
    def __init__(self, count: int) -> None:
        self._count = count

    def scan(self) -> Any:
        count = self._count

        class _Scan:
            def count(self) -> int:
                return count

        return _Scan()


_ICEBERG_CONFIG = {
    "catalog_type": "rest",
    "uri": "http://localhost:8181",
    "warehouse": "wh",
}


def test_iceberg_count_preflight_and_read(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        dataset_reader, "load_iceberg_table", lambda cfg, secret, ident: FakeIcebergTable(4)
    )
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        dataset_reader, "read_iceberg_dataframe", lambda cfg, secret, ident, **kw: _frame(4)
    )
    df = read_dataset(
        _conn("iceberg", secret_ref=None, **_ICEBERG_CONFIG),
        DatasetSpec(table="retail.orders"),
        max_rows=10,
        secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )
    assert len(df) == 4


def test_iceberg_over_cap_fails_before_materializing(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        dataset_reader, "load_iceberg_table", lambda cfg, secret, ident: FakeIcebergTable(11)
    )

    def _explode(*a: Any, **kw: Any) -> Any:
        raise AssertionError("must not materialize an over-cap iceberg table")

    monkeypatch.setattr(dataset_reader, "read_iceberg_dataframe", _explode)
    with pytest.raises(DatasetTooLargeError, match="retail.orders"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("iceberg", secret_ref=None, **_ICEBERG_CONFIG),
            DatasetSpec(table="retail.orders"),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_iceberg_requires_identifier() -> None:
    with pytest.raises(DatasetReadUnsupportedError, match="namespace.table"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("iceberg", secret_ref=None, **_ICEBERG_CONFIG),
            DatasetSpec(),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )

        id=uuid.uuid4(),
        name=f"{conn_type}-t",
        type=conn_type,
        env="dev",
        config=config,
        secret_ref=secret_ref,
        created_by=uuid.uuid4(),
    )


def _frame(rows: int) -> pd.DataFrame:
    return pd.DataFrame({"id": range(rows)})


class FakeSqlConnection:
    """Stands in for the SQLAlchemy connection `_open_connection` yields."""

    def __init__(self, count: int) -> None:
        self._count = count
        self.statements: list[str] = []

    def execute(self, stmt: Any) -> Any:
        self.statements.append(str(stmt))
        count = self._count

        class _Result:
            def scalar_one(self) -> int:
                return count

        return _Result()


def _patch_sql(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, *, count: int, frame: pd.DataFrame
) -> FakeSqlConnection:
    fake = FakeSqlConnection(count)

    @contextmanager
    def _fake_open(connection: Connection, secret_store: Any) -> Any:
        yield fake

    monkeypatch.setattr(dataset_reader, "_open_connection", _fake_open)
    monkeypatch.setattr(pd, "read_sql", lambda stmt, conn: frame)
    return fake


# ───────────────────────── dispatch ─────────────────────────────────


def test_orchestration_type_has_no_reader() -> None:
    with pytest.raises(DatasetReadUnsupportedError, match="orchestration"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("airflow"),
            DatasetSpec(table="t"),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_non_positive_cap_rejected() -> None:
    with pytest.raises(DatasetReadUnsupportedError, match="max_rows"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("snowflake"),
            DatasetSpec(table="t", schema="s"),
            max_rows=0,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_default_max_rows_reads_settings() -> None:
    assert dataset_reader.default_max_rows() == 100_000


# ───────────────────────── SQL path ─────────────────────────────────


def test_sql_table_read_happy_path(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    fake = _patch_sql(monkeypatch, count=3, frame=_frame(3))
    df = read_dataset(
        _conn("snowflake"),
        DatasetSpec(table="ORDERS", schema="RETAIL"),
        max_rows=10,
        secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )
    assert len(df) == 3
    # COUNT preflight ran before the read.
    assert "count" in fake.statements[0].lower()


def test_sql_count_preflight_fails_fast_without_reading(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    def _explode(stmt: Any, conn: Any) -> Any:
        raise AssertionError("read_sql must not run when the preflight is over-cap")

    fake = FakeSqlConnection(count=11)

    @contextmanager
    def _fake_open(connection: Connection, secret_store: Any) -> Any:
        yield fake

    monkeypatch.setattr(dataset_reader, "_open_connection", _fake_open)
    monkeypatch.setattr(pd, "read_sql", _explode)
    with pytest.raises(DatasetTooLargeError, match="11 rows"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("unity_catalog"),
            DatasetSpec(table="t", schema="s", catalog="c"),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_sql_post_read_race_guard(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    # COUNT said 10 (under cap) but rows landed before the read → the LIMIT
    # max_rows+1 read surfaces 11 rows → refuse, never diff a truncated frame.
    _patch_sql(monkeypatch, count=10, frame=_frame(11))
    with pytest.raises(DatasetTooLargeError):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("snowflake"),
            DatasetSpec(table="t", schema="s"),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_sql_query_spec_wraps_and_limits(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    fake = _patch_sql(monkeypatch, count=2, frame=_frame(2))
    df = read_dataset(
        _conn("snowflake"),
        DatasetSpec(query="SELECT id FROM RETAIL.ORDERS;"),
        max_rows=10,
        secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )
    assert len(df) == 2
    assert "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT id FROM RETAIL.ORDERS) __dataq_src" in fake.statements[0]


def test_sql_query_revalidated_read_only_at_read_time(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    # Defence in depth: even if a writeful query somehow reached storage, the
    # reader re-validates before interpolating it into the wrappers.
    _patch_sql(monkeypatch, count=1, frame=_frame(1))
    with pytest.raises(CustomSqlInvalidError):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("snowflake"),
            DatasetSpec(query="DELETE FROM ORDERS"),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


# ───────────────────────── flat-file path ───────────────────────────


def test_flatfile_read_and_cap(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        dataset_reader, "read_flatfile_dataframe", lambda **kw: _frame(5)
    )
    store = FakeSecretStore()
    df = read_dataset(
        _conn("s3", bucket="b"),
        DatasetSpec(path="orders.csv"),
        max_rows=5,
        secret_store=store,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )
    assert len(df) == 5 and store.requested == ["conn-x"]

    monkeypatch.setattr(
        dataset_reader, "read_flatfile_dataframe", lambda **kw: _frame(6)
    )
    with pytest.raises(DatasetTooLargeError, match="orders.csv"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("adls_gen2"),
            DatasetSpec(path="orders.csv"),
            max_rows=5,
            secret_store=store,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_flatfile_requires_secret_and_path() -> None:
    with pytest.raises(DatasetReadUnsupportedError, match="credential"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("s3", secret_ref=None),
            DatasetSpec(path="orders.csv"),
            max_rows=5,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )
    with pytest.raises(DatasetReadUnsupportedError, match="path"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("s3"),
            DatasetSpec(),
            max_rows=5,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


# ───────────────────────── iceberg path ─────────────────────────────


class FakeIcebergTable:
    def __init__(self, count: int) -> None:
        self._count = count

    def scan(self) -> Any:
        count = self._count

        class _Scan:
            def count(self) -> int:
                return count

        return _Scan()


_ICEBERG_CONFIG = {
    "catalog_type": "rest",
    "uri": "http://localhost:8181",
    "warehouse": "wh",
}


def test_iceberg_count_preflight_and_read(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        dataset_reader, "load_iceberg_table", lambda cfg, secret, ident: FakeIcebergTable(4)
    )
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        dataset_reader, "read_iceberg_dataframe", lambda cfg, secret, ident, **kw: _frame(4)
    )
    df = read_dataset(
        _conn("iceberg", secret_ref=None, **_ICEBERG_CONFIG),
        DatasetSpec(table="retail.orders"),
        max_rows=10,
        secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )
    assert len(df) == 4


def test_iceberg_over_cap_fails_before_materializing(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        dataset_reader, "load_iceberg_table", lambda cfg, secret, ident: FakeIcebergTable(11)
    )

    def _explode(*a: Any, **kw: Any) -> Any:
        raise AssertionError("must not materialize an over-cap iceberg table")

    monkeypatch.setattr(dataset_reader, "read_iceberg_dataframe", _explode)
    with pytest.raises(DatasetTooLargeError, match="retail.orders"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("iceberg", secret_ref=None, **_ICEBERG_CONFIG),
            DatasetSpec(table="retail.orders"),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_iceberg_requires_identifier() -> None:
    with pytest.raises(DatasetReadUnsupportedError, match="namespace.table"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("iceberg", secret_ref=None, **_ICEBERG_CONFIG),
            DatasetSpec(),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )

class IcebergConfig(BaseModel):
    """Non-secret Iceberg catalog + storage config (the credential is the secret).

    Maps from ``Connection.config``. ``catalog_name`` is the pyiceberg catalog
    name; ``catalog_type`` selects the backend; ``catalog_uri`` points at it
    (required for rest/sql/hive). ``warehouse`` is the table warehouse/storage
    root. ``properties`` carries any extra non-secret catalog + storage options
    (e.g. ``{"s3.region": "us-east-1", "adls.account-name": "acct"}``).
    ``secret_property`` names the single ``load_catalog`` property the connection's
    secret fills — the only place the credential lands.
    """

    model_config = ConfigDict(extra="forbid")

    catalog_name: str = "default"
    catalog_type: IcebergCatalogType
    catalog_uri: str | None = None
    warehouse: str | None = None
    properties: dict[str, str] = {}
    secret_property: str | None = None

    @model_validator(mode="after")
    def _uri_present(self) -> IcebergConfig:
        if self.catalog_type in _URI_REQUIRED and not self.catalog_uri:
            raise ValueError(f"catalog_uri is required for a {self.catalog_type!r} catalog")
        return self

    def catalog_properties(self, secret: str | None) -> dict[str, str]:
        """The keyword properties handed to ``pyiceberg.catalog.load_catalog``.

        The freeform ``properties`` go in **first** so the validated
        ``type``/``uri``/``warehouse`` overwrite (never get shadowed by) any
        collision — otherwise a stray ``properties={'type': …}`` would diverge from
        what the ``_uri_present`` validator reasoned about. The single secret under
        ``secret_property`` is applied last so it can't be shadowed either.
        """
        props: dict[str, str] = dict(self.properties)
        props["type"] = self.catalog_type
        if self.catalog_uri:
            props["uri"] = self.catalog_uri
        if self.warehouse:
            props["warehouse"] = self.warehouse
        if self.secret_property and secret is not None:
            props[self.secret_property] = secret
        return props

"""DatasetReader seam tests (ADR 0015, #792) — no live datasources.

The live I/O seams (`_open_connection`, the flat-file/iceberg read functions)
are monkeypatched exactly like the profiler's tests; what's under test is the
dispatch, the fail-fast row-cap discipline (COUNT preflight + post-read race
guard, never truncation), the read-time read-only re-validation, and the
credential handling.
"""

import uuid
from contextlib import contextmanager
from typing import Any

import pandas as pd
import pytest

from backend.app.db.models import Connection
from backend.app.services import dataset_reader
from backend.app.services.custom_sql import CustomSqlInvalidError
from backend.app.services.dataset_reader import (
    DatasetReadUnsupportedError,
    DatasetSpec,
    DatasetTooLargeError,
    read_dataset,
)


class FakeSecretStore:
    def __init__(self, secret: str | None = "s3cret") -> None:
        self._secret = secret
        self.requested: list[str] = []

    def get(self, ref: str) -> str:
        self.requested.append(ref)
        assert self._secret is not None
        return self._secret


def _conn(conn_type: str, *, secret_ref: str | None = "conn-x", **config: Any) -> Connection:
    return Connection(
        id=uuid.uuid4(),
        name=f"{conn_type}-t",
        type=conn_type,
        env="dev",
        config=config,
        secret_ref=secret_ref,
        created_by=uuid.uuid4(),
    )


def _frame(rows: int) -> pd.DataFrame:
    return pd.DataFrame({"id": range(rows)})


class FakeSqlConnection:
    """Stands in for the SQLAlchemy connection `_open_connection` yields."""

    def __init__(self, count: int) -> None:
        self._count = count
        self.statements: list[str] = []

    def execute(self, stmt: Any) -> Any:
        self.statements.append(str(stmt))
        count = self._count

        class _Result:
            def scalar_one(self) -> int:
                return count

        return _Result()


def _patch_sql(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, *, count: int, frame: pd.DataFrame
) -> FakeSqlConnection:
    fake = FakeSqlConnection(count)

    @contextmanager
    def _fake_open(connection: Connection, secret_store: Any) -> Any:
        yield fake

    monkeypatch.setattr(dataset_reader, "_open_connection", _fake_open)
    monkeypatch.setattr(pd, "read_sql", lambda stmt, conn: frame)
    return fake


# ───────────────────────── dispatch ─────────────────────────────────


def test_orchestration_type_has_no_reader() -> None:
    with pytest.raises(DatasetReadUnsupportedError, match="orchestration"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("airflow"),
            DatasetSpec(table="t"),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_non_positive_cap_rejected() -> None:
    with pytest.raises(DatasetReadUnsupportedError, match="max_rows"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("snowflake"),
            DatasetSpec(table="t", schema="s"),
            max_rows=0,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_default_max_rows_reads_settings() -> None:
    assert dataset_reader.default_max_rows() == 100_000


# ───────────────────────── SQL path ─────────────────────────────────


def test_sql_table_read_happy_path(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    fake = _patch_sql(monkeypatch, count=3, frame=_frame(3))
    df = read_dataset(
        _conn("snowflake"),
        DatasetSpec(table="ORDERS", schema="RETAIL"),
        max_rows=10,
        secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )
    assert len(df) == 3
    # COUNT preflight ran before the read.
    assert "count" in fake.statements[0].lower()


def test_sql_count_preflight_fails_fast_without_reading(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    def _explode(stmt: Any, conn: Any) -> Any:
        raise AssertionError("read_sql must not run when the preflight is over-cap")

    fake = FakeSqlConnection(count=11)

    @contextmanager
    def _fake_open(connection: Connection, secret_store: Any) -> Any:
        yield fake

    monkeypatch.setattr(dataset_reader, "_open_connection", _fake_open)
    monkeypatch.setattr(pd, "read_sql", _explode)
    with pytest.raises(DatasetTooLargeError, match="11 rows"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("unity_catalog"),
            DatasetSpec(table="t", schema="s", catalog="c"),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_sql_post_read_race_guard(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    # COUNT said 10 (under cap) but rows landed before the read → the LIMIT
    # max_rows+1 read surfaces 11 rows → refuse, never diff a truncated frame.
    _patch_sql(monkeypatch, count=10, frame=_frame(11))
    with pytest.raises(DatasetTooLargeError):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("snowflake"),
            DatasetSpec(table="t", schema="s"),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_sql_query_spec_wraps_and_limits(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    fake = _patch_sql(monkeypatch, count=2, frame=_frame(2))
    df = read_dataset(
        _conn("snowflake"),
        DatasetSpec(query="SELECT id FROM RETAIL.ORDERS;"),
        max_rows=10,
        secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )
    assert len(df) == 2
    assert "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT id FROM RETAIL.ORDERS) __dataq_src" in fake.statements[0]


def test_sql_query_revalidated_read_only_at_read_time(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    # Defence in depth: even if a writeful query somehow reached storage, the
    # reader re-validates before interpolating it into the wrappers.
    _patch_sql(monkeypatch, count=1, frame=_frame(1))
    with pytest.raises(CustomSqlInvalidError):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("snowflake"),
            DatasetSpec(query="DELETE FROM ORDERS"),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


# ───────────────────────── flat-file path ───────────────────────────


def test_flatfile_read_and_cap(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    monkeypatch.setattr(dataset_reader, "read_flatfile_dataframe", lambda **kw: _frame(5))
    store = FakeSecretStore()
    df = read_dataset(
        _conn("s3", bucket="b"),
        DatasetSpec(path="orders.csv"),
        max_rows=5,
        secret_store=store,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )
    assert len(df) == 5 and store.requested == ["conn-x"]

    monkeypatch.setattr(dataset_reader, "read_flatfile_dataframe", lambda **kw: _frame(6))
    with pytest.raises(DatasetTooLargeError, match="orders.csv"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("adls_gen2"),
            DatasetSpec(path="orders.csv"),
            max_rows=5,
            secret_store=store,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_flatfile_requires_secret_and_path() -> None:
    with pytest.raises(DatasetReadUnsupportedError, match="credential"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("s3", secret_ref=None),
            DatasetSpec(path="orders.csv"),
            max_rows=5,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )
    with pytest.raises(DatasetReadUnsupportedError, match="path"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("s3"),
            DatasetSpec(),
            max_rows=5,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


# ───────────────────────── iceberg path ─────────────────────────────


class FakeIcebergTable:
    def __init__(self, count: int) -> None:
        self._count = count

    def scan(self) -> Any:
        count = self._count

        class _Scan:
            def count(self) -> int:
                return count

        return _Scan()


_ICEBERG_CONFIG = {
    "catalog_type": "rest",
    "uri": "http://localhost:8181",
    "warehouse": "wh",
}


def test_iceberg_count_preflight_and_read(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        dataset_reader, "load_iceberg_table", lambda cfg, secret, ident: FakeIcebergTable(4)
    )
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        dataset_reader, "read_iceberg_dataframe", lambda cfg, secret, ident, **kw: _frame(4)
    )
    df = read_dataset(
        _conn("iceberg", secret_ref=None, **_ICEBERG_CONFIG),
        DatasetSpec(table="retail.orders"),
        max_rows=10,
        secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )
    assert len(df) == 4


def test_iceberg_over_cap_fails_before_materializing(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        dataset_reader, "load_iceberg_table", lambda cfg, secret, ident: FakeIcebergTable(11)
    )

    def _explode(*a: Any, **kw: Any) -> Any:
        raise AssertionError("must not materialize an over-cap iceberg table")

    monkeypatch.setattr(dataset_reader, "read_iceberg_dataframe", _explode)
    with pytest.raises(DatasetTooLargeError, match="retail.orders"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("iceberg", secret_ref=None, **_ICEBERG_CONFIG),
            DatasetSpec(table="retail.orders"),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_iceberg_requires_identifier() -> None:
    with pytest.raises(DatasetReadUnsupportedError, match="namespace.table"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("iceberg", secret_ref=None, **_ICEBERG_CONFIG),
            DatasetSpec(),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )

5	dispatch, the fail-fast row-cap discipline (COUNT preflight + post-read race
6	guard, never truncation), the read-time read-only re-validation, and the
7	credential handling.
8	"""

35	        self.requested.append(ref)

44	        type=conn_type,
45	        env="dev",

57	    """Stands in for the SQLAlchemy connection `_open_connection` yields."""
58	
59	    def __init__(self, count: int) -> None:
60	        self._count = count

62	
63	    def execute(self, stmt: Any) -> Any:
64	        self.statements.append(str(stmt))
65	        count = self._count
66	
67	        class _Result:

69	                return count
70	
71	        return _Result()

1	"""Read one comparison side (source or target) into a DataFrame (ADR 0015, #792).
2	
3	The `DatasetReader` seam: given a **datasource** connection and a `DatasetSpec`
4	(a resolved table triple, a flat-file path, or a read-only SQL projection),
5	return a bounded pandas DataFrame for the comparison engine (#793). Grown from
6	plumbing that already exists — the profiler's SQL engine access
7	(`profile_service`), `flatfile`'s object read, `pyiceberg`'s scan — behind the
8	same dict-dispatch shape as the profiler's `_PROFILERS`, so service code never
9	branches on `connection.type`. Orchestration types have no reader (a clean 422,
10	mirroring `build_check_runner`).
11	
12	Row-cap discipline (ADR 0015 §3): `max_rows` is enforced **fail-fast** — a
13	cheap COUNT preflight where the backend offers one (SQL engines, Iceberg scan
14	metadata) so an oversized table never transfers, plus a post-read length check
15	everywhere (the preflight is racy by nature; flat files have no preflight at
16	all). Over-cap raises `DatasetTooLargeError`, **never** a silent truncation —
17	a truncated diff produces confidently wrong mismatch buckets, which is worse
18	than no answer.
19	
20	FastAPI-free like the sibling services: takes ORM `Connection` + `SecretStore`,
21	returns DataFrames, raises `DataQError` subclasses (the #794 run path maps them
22	to operational `error` results; the authoring/dry-run paths surface them as
23	422s).
24	"""
25	
26	from __future__ import annotations
27	
28	from collections.abc import Callable
29	from dataclasses import dataclass
30	from typing import Any
31	
32	import sqlalchemy as sa
33	
34	from backend.app.core.config import get_settings
35	from backend.app.core.errors import DataQError
36	from backend.app.core.logging import get_logger
37	from backend.app.core.secrets import SecretStore
38	from backend.app.datasources.flatfile import read_dataframe as read_flatfile_dataframe
39	from backend.app.datasources.iceberg import (
40	    IcebergConfig,
41	    load_iceberg_table,
42	    read_iceberg_dataframe,
43	)
44	from backend.app.db.models import Connection
45	from backend.app.services.custom_sql import validate_query
46	from backend.app.services.profile_service import (
47	    _open_connection,
48	    _table,
49	    resolve_effective_schema,
50	)
51	
52	log = get_logger(__name__)
53	
54	
55	class DatasetReadUnsupportedError(DataQError):
56	    status_code = 422
57	    code = "comparison_read_unsupported"
58	
59	
60	class DatasetTooLargeError(DataQError):
61	    status_code = 422
62	    code = "comparison_dataset_too_large"
63	
64	
65	@dataclass(frozen=True)
66	class DatasetSpec:
67	    """What to read from one comparison side.
68	
69	    Exactly one addressing mode is used per datasource: `query` (SQL sources,
70	    already author-time validated read-only), the `table`/`schema`/`catalog`
71	    triple (SQL sources; `table` also carries the Iceberg `namespace.table`
72	    identifier), or `path` (flat-file sources — the caller materializes batch
73	    targets to a concrete path first, exactly like a run does).
74	    """
75	
76	    table: str | None = None
77	    schema: str | None = None
78	    catalog: str | None = None
79	    path: str | None = None
80	    query: str | None = None
81	
82	
83	def default_max_rows() -> int:
84	    """The settings-level comparison row cap (per-check `config.max_rows`
85	    overrides it — resolved by the caller, ADR 0015 §3)."""
86	    return get_settings().comparison_max_rows
87	
88	
89	def _too_large(count: int, max_rows: int, *, side_hint: str) -> DatasetTooLargeError:
90	    return DatasetTooLargeError(
91	        f"{side_hint} has {count} rows, over the comparison cap of {max_rows} — "
92	        "raise config.max_rows deliberately or narrow the dataset (a truncated "
93	        "diff would be silently wrong, so DataQ refuses instead)",
94	        detail={"rows": count, "max_rows": max_rows},
95	    )
96	
97	
98	def _require_secret(connection: Connection, secret_store: SecretStore) -> str:
99	    if not connection.secret_ref:
100	        raise DatasetReadUnsupportedError(
101	            "connection has no stored credential",
102	            detail={"connection_id": str(connection.id)},
103	        )
104	    return secret_store.get(connection.secret_ref)
105	
106	
107	# ───────────────────────── SQL (snowflake / unity_catalog) ──────────
108	
109	
110	def _wrapped_query(spec: DatasetSpec) -> str:
111	    """The validated read-only projection as a parenthesized FROM source.
112	
113	    Re-validated here (defence in depth — author-time validation already ran):
114	    a single read-only SELECT/WITH statement, so interpolating it into the
115	    COUNT/LIMIT wrappers below cannot smuggle a second statement or a write.
116	    """
117	    assert spec.query is not None
118	    validate_query(spec.query)
119	    return spec.query.rstrip().rstrip(";")
120	
121	
122	def _sql_read(
123	    connection: Connection, spec: DatasetSpec, max_rows: int, secret_store: SecretStore
124	) -> Any:
125	    import pandas as pd
126	
127	    count_stmt: Any
128	    select_stmt: Any
129	    if spec.query is not None:
130	        q = _wrapped_query(spec)
131	        # Interpolation is safe: `q` passed the read-only single-statement
132	        # validator (ADR 0019) at author time AND immediately above.
133	        count_stmt = sa.text(f"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ({q}) __dataq_src")  # noqa: S608
134	        select_stmt = sa.text(
135	            f"SELECT * FROM ({q}) __dataq_src LIMIT {int(max_rows) + 1}"  # noqa: S608
136	        )
137	    else:
138	        schema = resolve_effective_schema(connection, spec.schema)
139	        source = _table(schema, spec.table or "", spec.catalog)
140	        count_stmt = sa.select(sa.func.count()).select_from(source)
141	        select_stmt = sa.select(sa.text("*")).select_from(source).limit(max_rows + 1)
142	
143	    with _open_connection(connection, secret_store) as conn:
144	        count = int(conn.execute(count_stmt).scalar_one())
145	        if count > max_rows:
146	            raise _too_large(count, max_rows, side_hint="dataset")
147	        df = pd.read_sql(select_stmt, conn)
148	    # Belt over the braces: the COUNT is racy (rows can land between preflight
149	    # and read) and the LIMIT is max_rows+1 exactly so growth is detectable.
150	    if len(df) > max_rows:
151	        raise _too_large(len(df), max_rows, side_hint="dataset")
152	    return df
153	
154	
155	# ───────────────────────── flat file (adls_gen2 / s3) ───────────────
156	
157	
158	def _flatfile_read(
159	    connection: Connection, spec: DatasetSpec, max_rows: int, secret_store: SecretStore
160	) -> Any:
161	    path = spec.path or spec.table
162	    if not path:
163	        raise DatasetReadUnsupportedError(
164	            "flat-file comparison side needs a resolved path", detail={"spec": "path"}
165	        )
166	    secret = _require_secret(connection, secret_store)
167	    # No cheap preflight exists for an object read — the whole file downloads,
168	    # then the cap is enforced before any diffing.
169	    df = read_flatfile_dataframe(
170	        conn_type=connection.type, config=dict(connection.config), path=path, secret=secret
171	    )
172	    if len(df) > max_rows:
173	        raise _too_large(len(df), max_rows, side_hint=f"file {path!r}")
174	    return df
175	
176	
177	# ───────────────────────── iceberg (native pyiceberg) ───────────────
178	
179	
180	def _iceberg_read(
181	    connection: Connection, spec: DatasetSpec, max_rows: int, secret_store: SecretStore
182	) -> Any:
183	    if not spec.table:
184	        raise DatasetReadUnsupportedError(
185	            "iceberg comparison side needs a namespace.table identifier",
186	            detail={"spec": "table"},
187	        )
188	    cfg = IcebergConfig.model_validate(connection.config)
189	    # Credential optional, mirroring `build_iceberg_runner` (a local warehouse /
190	    # vended-credentials REST catalog has none).
191	    secret = secret_store.get(connection.secret_ref) if connection.secret_ref else None
192	    table = load_iceberg_table(cfg, secret, spec.table)

Follow-ups filed to `v1.1 Backlog`: [#682](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/682) (webhook-auth metadata onto the `OrchestrationProvider` seam) + [#685](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/685) (version-history purge/redact path). — **#524 + #589 closed via [#628](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/628)** — opencensus→OTel log migration + generic OTLP endpoint, both behind a new shared `otel.py` exporter seam; agentic review (code-reviewer + silent-failure-hunter) folded in; **deployed + parity live-verified** (local OTLP Collector received spans+logs redacted over the wire; prod App Insights `traces` now carry `cloud_RoleName=dataq-api/worker` via the OTel exporter, marker probe traced end-to-end). W1 progress 2026-07-04/05: #194/#195/#587 closed via #602/#603/#607; **#461 closed via #613** (PATs phase 1 merged — live PAT mint pending prod deploy) and **#583 via #614**; #588 rehearsed→reversed→reopened on W3; **#609 closed 2026-07-05** (self-hosted dbt Core lineage — dbt build green local + ACA Job, DataQ suite green on a dynamic table, artifacts to ADLS; unblocks #611) + **#611 closed 2026-07-05** (dbt as a third `OrchestrationProvider` — ADR 0029, #620; deployed to prod + **webhook live-verified**: signed event → pipeline_run → bound mart suite fired green; poll live-read deferred to #622) + **#372 + #622 closed 2026-07-05** (SecretStore.delete #625 + custom least-priv KV get/list/set/soft-delete role #624 — both deployed & prod-verified: connection-delete soft-deletes its secret) + #286 pulled W7→W2; [#604](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/604) — CI-flaky ConnectionNew test — filed and closed same-day by #603; filed still-open: [#601](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/601) prettierignore gap + [#605](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/605) surface run failure reasons, both `v1.1 Backlog`). At the 2026-07-04 full backlog remap: **55 scheduled** onto `v1.1 Week 1..6` + **10** on `v1.1 Week 7 — stretch` + the cycle epic [#597](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/597). **`v1.1 Backlog` (renamed from `Backlog (post-v1 / testing)`) held only the new filings #601/#605 at the remap** — every other then-open issue sat on a week milestone; the backlog milestone is the default for new filings and has since re-filled (incl. the G-d build set #757–#762). Every scheduled issue carries an **Acceptance criteria** checklist and every milestone description its **Exit gate** (both added 2026-07-04). Theme map in [post-v1-roadmap.md](../context/post-v1-roadmap.md). |

Same session: **#796 CI-E2E 429 regression fixed via [#797](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/797)** — the #783 limiter throttled the tokenless dev-bypass Playwright suite (whole suite = one per-IP `unauth` bucket); diagnosed from the report artifact ("Too many requests" rendered in the failing pages); `RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=false` in the CI E2E job + compose dev default. · **#725 request rate limiting shipped (PR #783)** — app-level *innermost* HTTP middleware on every public surface (REST + orchestration webhooks + the `/mcp` mount a route dependency can't reach); fixed-window Redis counter (window index in the key → no EXPIRE race), **no new dep** (`redis` INCR over slowapi/limits), **fail-open**; endpoint classes `webhook`(per-IP) · `default`(per-`sha256(token)`) · `unauth`(per-IP), raw token never keyed/logged; `/healthz`+`OPTIONS` exempt; 429 carries `Retry-After` + `detail.retry_after_seconds`; ADR [0035](adr/0035-request-rate-limiting.md); the named hard prereq for the ADR-0032 OTP sign-in (#738). · **#773 nav inversion shipped (this PR)** — G-d phase 4: the dashboard opens with an asset-health lead strip (monitored / need-attention / in-progress, severity-sorted preview, explicit first-200 truncation note), Assets moves to the top of the sidebar, suite/run detail link back to their asset, and admins get an owner-reassignment picker on AssetDetail (mount-gated so the /me-resolution race can't strand it; `/admin/users` never fetched for non-admins — structural + test-pinned). UC/flat-file/Iceberg: Python value type name — verified per-runner execution engine, not guessed) + a [datasources-checks.md](datasources-checks.md) cheat-sheet; the dry-run preview already surfaced `observed_value` for calibration, so no new plumbing there. **G-d build COMPLETE — #762 LineageProvider/Marquez shipped** (the seam + pull + compose profile; all six G-d issues #596/#757–#762 now closed) · **#761 incidents shipped** (G-d phase 3 — lifecycle+dedup engine, evidence card, publisher payload, grant-derived incident API + AssetDetail panel; #773 nav-inversion filed as phase 4) · **W3 in progress 2026-07-11** — **#767 P1 result-misattribution fixed** ([PR #771](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/771) — GX error-first reorder vs positional zip; MCP tool explicitly deferred). [#654](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/654) connection version-history drawer on the edit page ([#683](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/683)); [#653](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/653) per-check alert snooze/unsnooze in the suite detail panel ([#684](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/684) + follow-ups [#687](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/687)); [#651](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/651) author-time GX-registry validation of expectation-kind checks ([#686](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/686) + review follow-ups [#689](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/689)→[#690](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/690): dict-key size gap, bounded 422 echo, 1k→10k cap, PATCH no longer bricks pre-#651 legacy rows); [#617](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/617) mobile/responsive pass ([#688](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/688) — suite-detail stacks below `md`, tables get `scroll={{x}}`, Sider-trigger/header overlap fixed); **residual [#692](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/692) closed via [#693](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/693)** (user-flagged after #688: the checks-list `SimpleList.Item` rows still collapsed to char-per-line at ≤360px because the never-shrinking actions squeezed the content — fixed in the shim with `flex-wrap` + a `min(100%,180px)` content floor so actions drop to their own line; header brand `nowrap`; live-verified 320/390/1280 + `SimpleList.test.tsx`). Follow-ups filed to `v1.1 Backlog`: [#682](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/682) (webhook-auth metadata onto the `OrchestrationProvider` seam) + [#685](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/685) (version-history purge/redact path). — **#524 + #589 closed via [#628](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/628)** — opencensus→OTel log migration + generic OTLP endpoint, both behind a new shared `otel.py` exporter seam; agentic review (code-reviewer + silent-failure-hunter) folded in; **deployed + parity live-verified** (local OTLP Collector received spans+logs redacted over the wire; prod App Insights `traces` now carry `cloud_RoleName=dataq-api/worker` via the OTel exporter, marker probe traced end-to-end). W1 progress 2026-07-04/05: #194/#195/#587 closed via #602/#603/#607; **#461 closed via #613** (PATs phase 1 merged — live PAT mint pending prod deploy) and **#583 via #614**; #588 rehearsed→reversed→reopened on W3; **#609 closed 2026-07-05** (self-hosted dbt Core lineage — dbt build green local + ACA Job, DataQ suite green on a dynamic table, artifacts to ADLS; unblocks #611) + **#611 closed 2026-07-05** (dbt as a third `OrchestrationProvider` — ADR 0029, #620; deployed to prod + **webhook live-verified**: signed event → pipeline_run → bound mart suite fired green; poll live-read deferred to #622) + **#372 + #622 closed 2026-07-05** (SecretStore.delete #625 + custom least-priv KV get/list/set/soft-delete role #624 — both deployed & prod-verified: connection-delete soft-deletes its secret) + #286 pulled W7→W2; [#604](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/604) — CI-flaky ConnectionNew test — filed and closed same-day by #603; filed still-open: [#601](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/601) prettierignore gap + [#605](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/605) surface run failure reasons, both `v1.1 Backlog`). At the 2026-07-04 full backlog remap: **55 scheduled** onto `v1.1 Week 1..6` + **10** on `v1.1 Week 7 — stretch` + the cycle epic [#597](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/597). **`v1.1 Backlog` (renamed from `Backlog (post-v1 / testing)`) held only the new filings #601/#605 at the remap** — every other then-open issue sat on a week milestone; the backlog milestone is the default for new filings and has since re-filled (incl. the G-d build set #757–#762). Every scheduled issue carries an **Acceptance criteria** checklist and every milestone description its **Exit gate** (both added 2026-07-04). Closes the design gate reserved since ADR 0014; build set [#791](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/791)–[#795](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/795) filed on W3. **#792 DatasetReader seam shipped (this PR)** — `services/dataset_reader.py`: per-datasource capped DataFrame reads behind profiler-style dict dispatch (SQL via the profiler engine plumbing, flat-file object read, `pyiceberg` scan; orchestration types 422), COUNT/scan-metadata preflight + post-read race guard + typed `DatasetTooLargeError` (fail-fast, never truncation), read-time read-only re-validation of query specs, `COMPARISON_MAX_ROWS` setting (default 100k, per-check `config.max_rows` override) documented in deploy/README. **#791 schema + authoring slice shipped ([#798](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/798))** — `checks.source_connection_id` FK + kind⇔presence CHECK (migration `b7c8d9e0f1a2`, up+down tested), author-time validation (source spec via `resolve_target`, read-only per-side SQL per ADR 0019, join keys, orchestration sources rejected), RESTRICT delete guard (409 `connection_in_use`), export/import `(name, env)` round-trip, version snapshots, and the run-path guard (a comparison check yields a per-check `error` result until the #794 runner — never fails its siblings' run). Same session: **#796 CI-E2E 429 regression fixed via [#797](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/797)** — the #783 limiter throttled the tokenless dev-bypass Playwright suite (whole suite = one per-IP `unauth` bucket); diagnosed from the report artifact ("Too many requests" rendered in the failing pages); `RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=false` in the CI E2E job + compose dev default. · **#725 request rate limiting shipped (PR #783)** — app-level *innermost* HTTP middleware on every public surface (REST + orchestration webhooks + the `/mcp` mount a route dependency can't reach); fixed-window Redis counter (window index in the key → no EXPIRE race), **no new dep** (`redis` INCR over slowapi/limits), **fail-open**; endpoint classes `webhook`(per-IP) · `default`(per-`sha256(token)`) · `unauth`(per-IP), raw token never keyed/logged; `/healthz`+`OPTIONS` exempt; 429 carries `Retry-After` + `detail.retry_after_seconds`; ADR [0035](adr/0035-request-rate-limiting.md); the named hard prereq for the ADR-0032 OTP sign-in (#738). · **#773 nav inversion shipped (this PR)** — G-d phase 4: the dashboard opens with an asset-health lead strip (monitored / need-attention / in-progress, severity-sorted preview, explicit first-200 truncation note), Assets moves to the top of the sidebar, suite/run detail link back to their asset, and admins get an owner-reassignment picker on AssetDetail (mount-gated so the /me-resolution race can't strand it; `/admin/users` never fetched for non-admins — structural + test-pinned). UC/flat-file/Iceberg: Python value type name — verified per-runner execution engine, not guessed) + a [datasources-checks.md](datasources-checks.md) cheat-sheet; the dry-run preview already surfaced `observed_value` for calibration, so no new plumbing there. **G-d build COMPLETE — #762 LineageProvider/Marquez shipped** (the seam + pull + compose profile;
