lines: | **v1 baseline** | `v1.0.0` tagged 2026-07-04 — 187/189 roadmap tasks (~99%); all 8 weekly exit gates met; deployed to Azure Container Apps; retro at [retro-v1.md](retro-v1.md); full ledger at [progress-v1.md](progress-v1.md) |

| **Current cycle** | **v1.1 — 6 weeks + a stretch week, 2026-07-04 → 2026-08-15 (+ W7 stretch to 2026-08-22)** (planned 2026-07-04 from [context/post-v1-roadmap.md](../context/post-v1-roadmap.md)). Sequencing is **subscription-driven**: Weeks 1–3 extract everything that needs the expiring Snowflake (lapses within days) and Azure (~2026-07-25) subscriptions, then wind down gracefully; Weeks 4–6 run the roadmap's recommended opening sequence (Theme-1 `schema_drift` + `anomaly` → scale-aware execution G-b → incident/lineage design G-d — the G-d design half, #596, was pulled forward and done in W3, 2026-07-10) on cloud-independent infra; W7 is the stretch/burn-down buffer. See [Cycle plan](#cycle-plan--v11-6-weeks--stretch-2026-07-04--2026-08-22) below. |

-| **Open issues** | **77** (**ADR [0015](adr/0015-two-connection-comparison-check-model.md) written + merged ([#790](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/790), 2026-07-12)** — the two-connection comparison check model: the suite stays single-connection and supplies the **target under test**; a `comparison` check adds one **source** ref. Closes the design gate reserved since ADR 0014; build set [#791](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/791)–[#795](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/795) filed on W3. **#792 DatasetReader seam shipped (this PR)** — `services/dataset_reader.py`: per-datasource capped DataFrame reads behind profiler-style dict dispatch (SQL via the profiler engine plumbing, flat-file object read, `pyiceberg` scan; orchestration types 422), COUNT/scan-metadata preflight + post-read race guard + typed `DatasetTooLargeError` (fail-fast, never truncation), read-time read-only re-validation of query specs, `COMPARISON_MAX_ROWS` setting (default 100k, per-check `config.max_rows` override) documented in deploy/README. **#791 schema + authoring slice shipped ([#798](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/798))** — `checks.source_connection_id` FK + kind⇔presence CHECK (migration `b7c8d9e0f1a2`, up+down tested), author-time validation (source spec via `resolve_target`, read-only per-side SQL per ADR 0019, join keys, orchestration sources rejected), RESTRICT delete guard (409 `connection_in_use`), export/import `(name, env)` round-trip, version snapshots, and the run-path guard (a comparison check yields a per-check `error` result until the #794 runner — never fails its siblings' run). Same session: **#796 CI-E2E 429 regression fixed via [#797](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/797)** — the #783 limiter throttled the tokenless dev-bypass Playwright suite (whole suite = one per-IP `unauth` bucket); diagnosed from the report artifact ("Too many requests" rendered in the failing pages); `RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=false` in the CI E2E job + compose dev default. · **#725 request rate limiting shipped (PR #783)** — app-level *innermost* HTTP middleware on every public surface (REST + orchestration webhooks + the `/mcp` mount a route dependency can't reach); fixed-window Redis counter (window index in the key → no EXPIRE race), **no new dep** (`redis` INCR over slowapi/limits), **fail-open**; endpoint classes `webhook`(per-IP) · `default`(per-`sha256(token)`) · `unauth`(per-IP), raw token never keyed/logged; `/healthz`+`OPTIONS` exempt; 429 carries `Retry-After` + `detail.retry_after_seconds`; ADR [0035](adr/0035-request-rate-limiting.md); the named hard prereq for the ADR-0032 OTP sign-in (#738). · **#773 nav inversion shipped (this PR)** — G-d phase 4: the dashboard opens with an asset-health lead strip (monitored / need-attention / in-progress, severity-sorted preview, explicit first-200 truncation note), Assets moves to the top of the sidebar, suite/run detail link back to their asset, and admins get an owner-reassignment picker on AssetDetail (mount-gated so the /me-resolution race can't strand it; `/admin/users` never fetched for non-admins — structural + test-pinned). Frontend-only — the owner PATCH backend had already shipped in #772. Same session: **UC dbt lineage live-verified PASS** (real `dbt-databricks` build on the live workspace → manifest v12 → `refresh_dbt_lineage` → correct 2-edge chain; the run-stamped UC asset and the dbt node resolved to the SAME row, provenance preserved; blast radius correct both directions — closes the feature-matrix's "adapter-aware, not live-tested" caveat) and the **AssetDetail CI flake fixed** (#778 via [#780](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/780), 15s budgets). · **#768 check-editor type guidance closed** — the `expect_column_values_to_be_of_type` UX finding from the #767 live schema-drift session: added the expectation to the check-editor catalog with a datasource-tailored `type_` hint (Snowflake: exact SQL dialect type; UC/flat-file/Iceberg: Python value type name — verified per-runner execution engine, not guessed) + a [datasources-checks.md](datasources-checks.md) cheat-sheet; the dry-run preview already surfaced `observed_value` for calibration, so no new plumbing there. **G-d build COMPLETE — #762 LineageProvider/Marquez shipped** (the seam + pull + compose profile; all six G-d issues #596/#757–#762 now closed) · **#761 incidents shipped** (G-d phase 3 — lifecycle+dedup engine, evidence card, publisher payload, grant-derived incident API + AssetDetail panel; #773 nav-inversion filed as phase 4) · **W3 in progress 2026-07-11** — **#767 P1 result-misattribution fixed** ([PR #771](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/771) — GX error-first reorder vs positional zip; found + fixed off the **2026-07-10/11 live-prod-DB test session**: schema-drift 6/6 detected on SF+UC, #759 add/drop-view lineage propagation proven E2E, dbt `artifacts_uri` docs footgun [#769](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/769); filed [#768](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/768) type-calibration UX + [#770](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/770) orphan-asset sweep; leaked UC PAT rotated same-session). **#760 asset view shipped** (G-d phase 2 — `/assets` read API + Assets pages, grant-derived authz, `asset_id` on Suite/RunRead; MCP tool explicitly deferred). Earlier — **#759 dbt-manifest lineage shipped** (`lineage_edges` cache + namespace-anchored asset mapping + blast-radius BFS + webhook/poll refresh; the refresh-propagation AC proven v1→v2), **#758 OpenLineage emission shipped** (dark-by-default `app/lineage` emitter + DQ facets, E2E-verified over real HTTP; the second G-d build issue) and **#757 asset entity built** (G-d phase 1 — `assets` table + resolver + backfill + run stamping; the first #596 build issue, pulled from `v1.1 Backlog`) right after the **#596 G-d design doc landed** (pulled forward from W6): [post-v1-assets-lineage-incidents-notes.md](post-v1-assets-lineage-incidents-notes.md) + ADR [0034](adr/0034-asset-entity-openlineage-identity-lineage-pull.md), with the phase-1 build set [#757](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/757)–[#762](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/762) filed to `v1.1 Backlog` as v1.2 generator input. Same day, the all-datasource perf baseline was captured in the last cloud window ([perf-baseline.md](perf-baseline.md); Snowflake pushdown green to 200M, full-load ceilings measured; findings [#753](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/753)/[#754](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/754)/[#755](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/755) filed). Earlier (2026-07-08): the Theme-10 refactor batch merged ([#720](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/720): closed #197/#199/#204/#229/#236/#326 — the last five were manually closed 2026-07-08, GitHub's `Closes` keyword only auto-closed #197 as the first item in the comma list; #237 closed obsolete) and **native Iceberg #716** landed (ADR 0030 impl — adapter + runner + registry + target + migration + connection/target UI; profiler follow-up [#721](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/721) filed); the wind-down trio #590/#591 + pre-teardown E2E stays deferred to the ~2026-07-25 lapse. — **W2 COMPLETE — milestone closed 2026-07-08**; exit gate MET 14/14, **all 5 in-week follow-ups cleared** (#571 [#699](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/699) run-detail checks_total graft, #640 [#700](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/700) flaky test, #643 [#701](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/701) stale-policy event, #605 [#702](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/702) redaction-safe run failure_reason); **#286 Iceberg spike DONE 2026-07-07** (ADR 0030 — engine-level read free/zero-code, native `pyiceberg` v2 read proven green, self-contained `iceberg` connection; native build → #716, v3 revisit → #717). Earlier: **#488 closed via [#695](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/695)** — workspace-admin workspace-wide visibility threaded into the MCP list/aggregate tools + `schedule_service.list_schedules`/`/schedules` (ADR-0027 REST parity; admin+non-admin authz tests); **#584 closed** — MCP NL tool-selection spot-check passed against live `/mcp` (VS Code Copilot Agent mode + W1 PAT routed all 4 canonical queries correctly, no docstring misses); **#532 closed via [#697](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/697)** — dry-run preview extended to all datasources via the `build_check_runner` registry + server-side target resolution (2-reviewer agentic review, 500→502 regression + batch-UI gap fixed in-PR). Earlier, W2 2026-07-05 — **alerting batch**: #386 (dedup severity single-source) + #387 (suppression guard) + #388 (alert_on drift-guard) closed via [#656](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/656); #389 closed won't-fix (Slack/email publishers made the per-channel name correct); **#655 closed via [#657](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/657)** — one canonical `db.models.SEVERITY_RANK` + `FAILING_TIERS` + shared `worst_severity()` helper (converged the 3 rank maps + the builder/run_service worst-severity loops); **#416 closed via [#659](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/659)** — enriched Slack/email alerts (deep link + metadata + expected-vs-observed via a shared pure `alerting/render.py`); **follow-up #661 closed via [#662](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/662)** — fully **tabular email** (run-details + failing-checks tables) + **owner name** across Slack/email/Teams (Teams card brought to #416 parity: metadata facts + "View run" action). #416 + #661 **deployed + live-verified in prod** (fresh non-deduped failing run → Slack + email delivered, enriched format confirmed). — **UI-gap remediation batch** (user-reported: "these aren't in the UI"): six gaps where a backend feature shipped without its frontend, each built → agentic-reviewed → merged → **[#630](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/630) dbt connection UI** ([#637](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/637)), **[#631](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/631) PAT management UI** ([#636](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/636)), **[#632](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/632) stale RunNowPanel stub** ([#638](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/638)), **[#633](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/633) per-suite Slack/email** (full-stack: migration `d2e3f4a5b6c7` + both publishers + panel, [#639](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/639)), **[#635](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/635) policy-panel catalog dropdown** ([#641](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/641)), **[#634](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/634) auto-classify column-policy on suite create** (async, fail-soft, [#642](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/642)); follow-ups filed [#640](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/640) (flaky LiveRunProgress) + [#643](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/643) (stale policy on target change). **Deployed to prod 2026-07-05** (run 28757740586, sha b394a889): the migrate job applied `d2e3f4a5b6c7` (the #633 `suite_notifications` columns) then rolled api/worker/frontend — prod healthy (frontend 200, api 401 auth-enforced), App Insights clean (no schema/column errors). A **`ui-tester` agent** (desktop+mobile UI + backend↔frontend parity audit) was added ([#645](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/645)) to catch this gap class going forward. — **Second UI-gap batch (user-flagged + agent-sweep findings, merged 2026-07-06, each agentic-reviewed):** [#618](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/618) stale "Azure AD (MSAL)" label → label derived from the runtime `authMode` + MSAL comment-rot scrub ([#679](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/679)); [#647](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/647) admin webhook-config mislabeled dbt rows as airflow → per-provider dispatch, `DBT_WEBHOOK_SECRET_NAME` documented ([#680](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/680)); [#652](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/652) dbt in the Suite-Triggers dropdown + Pipeline-runs filter + admin Webhooks labels ([#681](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/681)); [#654](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/654) connection version-history drawer on the edit page ([#683](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/683)); [#653](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/653) per-check alert snooze/unsnooze in the suite detail panel ([#684](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/684) + follow-ups [#687](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/687)); [#651](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/651) author-time GX-registry validation of expectation-kind checks ([#686](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/686) + review follow-ups [#689](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/689)→[#690](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/690): dict-key size gap, bounded 422 echo, 1k→10k cap, PATCH no longer bricks pre-#651 legacy rows); [#617](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/617) mobile/responsive pass ([#688](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/688) — suite-detail stacks below `md`, tables get `scroll={{x}}`, Sider-trigger/header overlap fixed); **residual [#692](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/692) closed via [#693](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/693)** (user-flagged after #688: the checks-list `SimpleList.Item` rows still collapsed to char-per-line at ≤360px because the never-shrinking actions squeezed the content — fixed in the shim with `flex-wrap` + a `min(100%,180px)` content floor so actions drop to their own line; header brand `nowrap`; live-verified 320/390/1280 + `SimpleList.test.tsx`). Follow-ups filed to `v1.1 Backlog`: [#682](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/682) (webhook-auth metadata onto the `OrchestrationProvider` seam) + [#685](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/685) (version-history purge/redact path). — **#524 + #589 closed via [#628](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/628)** — opencensus→OTel log migration + generic OTLP endpoint, both behind a new shared `otel.py` exporter seam; agentic review (code-reviewer + silent-failure-hunter) folded in; **deployed + parity live-verified** (local OTLP Collector received spans+logs redacted over the wire; prod App Insights `traces` now carry `cloud_RoleName=dataq-api/worker` via the OTel exporter, marker probe traced end-to-end). W1 progress 2026-07-04/05: #194/#195/#587 closed via #602/#603/#607; **#461 closed via #613** (PATs phase 1 merged — live PAT mint pending prod deploy) and **#583 via #614**; #588 rehearsed→reversed→reopened on W3; **#609 closed 2026-07-05** (self-hosted dbt Core lineage — dbt build green local + ACA Job, DataQ suite green on a dynamic table, artifacts to ADLS; unblocks #611) + **#611 closed 2026-07-05** (dbt as a third `OrchestrationProvider` — ADR 0029, #620; deployed to prod + **webhook live-verified**: signed event → pipeline_run → bound mart suite fired green; poll live-read deferred to #622) + **#372 + #622 closed 2026-07-05** (SecretStore.delete #625 + custom least-priv KV get/list/set/soft-delete role #624 — both deployed & prod-verified: connection-delete soft-deletes its secret) + #286 pulled W7→W2; [#604](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/604) — CI-flaky ConnectionNew test — filed and closed same-day by #603; filed still-open: [#601](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/601) prettierignore gap + [#605](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/605) surface run failure reasons, both `v1.1 Backlog`). At the 2026-07-04 full backlog remap: **55 scheduled** onto `v1.1 Week 1..6` + **10** on `v1.1 Week 7 — stretch` + the cycle epic [#597](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/issues/597). **`v1.1 Backlog` (renamed from `Backlog (post-v1 / testing)`) held only the new filings #601/#605 at the remap** — every other then-open issue sat on a week milestone; the backlog milestone is the default for new filings and has since re-filled (incl. the G-d build set #757–#762). Every scheduled issue carries an **Acceptance criteria** checklist and every milestone description its **Exit gate** (both added 2026-07-04). Theme map in [post-v1-roadmap.md](../context/post-v1-roadmap.md). |

Same session: **#796 CI-E2E 429 regression fixed via [#797](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/797)** — the #783 limiter throttled the tokenless dev-bypass Playwright suite (whole suite = one per-IP `unauth` bucket); diagnosed from the report artifact ("Too many requests" rendered in the failing pages); `RATE_LIMIT_ENABLED=false` in the CI E2E job + compose dev default. · **#725 request rate limiting shipped (PR #783)** — app-level *innermost* HTTP middleware on every public surface (REST + orchestration webhooks + the `/mcp` mount a route dependency can't reach); fixed-window Redis counter (window index in the key → no EXPIRE race), **no new dep** (`redis` INCR over slowapi/limits), **fail-open**; endpoint classes `webhook`(per-IP) · `default`(per-`sha256(token)`) · `unauth`(per-IP), raw token never keyed/logged; `/healthz`+`OPTIONS` exempt; 429 carries `Retry-After` + `detail.retry_after_seconds`; ADR [0035](adr/0035-request-rate-limiting.md); the named hard prereq for the ADR-0032 OTP sign-in (#738). · **#773 nav inversion shipped (this PR)** — G-d phase 4: the dashboard opens with an asset-health lead strip (monitored / need-attention / in-progress, severity-sorted preview, explicit first-200 truncation note), Assets moves to the top of the sidebar, suite/run detail link back to their asset, and admins get an owner-reassignment picker on AssetDetail (mount-gated so the /me-resolution race can't strand it; `/admin/users` never fetched for non-admins — structural + test-pinned). input.old_string:
def _wrapped_query(spec: DatasetSpec) -> str:
    """The validated read-only projection as a parenthesized FROM source.

    Re-validated here (defence in depth — author-time validation already ran):
    a single read-only SELECT/WITH statement, so interpolating it into the
    COUNT/LIMIT wrappers below cannot smuggle a second statement or a write.
    """
    assert spec.query is not None
    validate_query(spec.query)
    return spec.query.rstrip().rstrip(";")


def _sql_read(
    connection: Connection, spec: DatasetSpec, max_rows: int, secret_store: SecretStore
) -> Any:
    import pandas as pd

    count_stmt: Any
    select_stmt: Any
    if spec.query is not None:
        q = _wrapped_query(spec)
        # Interpolation is safe: `q` passed the read-only single-statement
        # validator (ADR 0019) at author time AND immediately above.
        count_stmt = sa.text(f"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ({q}) __dataq_src")  # noqa: S608
        select_stmt = sa.text(
            f"SELECT * FROM ({q}) __dataq_src LIMIT {int(max_rows) + 1}"  # noqa: S608
        )
    else:
        schema = resolve_effective_schema(connection, spec.schema)
        source = _table(schema, spec.table or "", spec.catalog)
        count_stmt = sa.select(sa.func.count()).select_from(source)
        select_stmt = sa.select(sa.text("*")).select_from(source).limit(max_rows + 1)

    with _open_connection(connection, secret_store) as conn:
        count = int(conn.execute(count_stmt).scalar_one())
        if count > max_rows:
            raise _too_large(count, max_rows, side_hint="dataset")
        df = pd.read_sql(select_stmt, conn)
    # Belt over the braces: the COUNT is racy (rows can land between preflight
    # and read) and the LIMIT is max_rows+1 exactly so growth is detectable.
    if len(df) > max_rows:
        raise _too_large(len(df), max_rows, side_hint="dataset")
    return df
input.new_string:
def _wrapped_query(spec: DatasetSpec) -> str:
    """The validated read-only projection as a parenthesized FROM source.

    Re-validated here (defence in depth — author-time validation already ran):
    a single read-only SELECT/WITH statement, so interpolating it into the
    COUNT/LIMIT wrappers below cannot smuggle a second statement or a write.
    The whitespace+semicolon tail is stripped in ONE pass (matching
    `validate_query`'s own trailing-chars rule — `.rstrip().rstrip(";")` would
    leave `SELECT 1; ` from a `; ;` tail and break the wrapper).
    """
    assert spec.query is not None
    validate_query(spec.query)
    return spec.query.strip().rstrip(string.whitespace + ";")


def _sql_read(
    connection: Connection, spec: DatasetSpec, max_rows: int, secret_store: SecretStore
) -> Any:
    import pandas as pd

    if not connection.secret_ref:
        # Pre-check so a credential-less connection is the same clean 422 the
        # flat-file path gives, not `_open_connection`'s bare ValueError 500.
        raise DatasetReadUnsupportedError(
            "connection has no stored credential",
            detail={"connection_id": str(connection.id)},
        )
    count_stmt: Any
    select_stmt: Any
    if spec.query is not None:
        q = _wrapped_query(spec)
        # Interpolation is safe: `q` passed the read-only single-statement
        # validator (ADR 0019) at author time AND immediately above. The
        # newline before the closing paren keeps a trailing `-- comment` (legal
        # per the validator) from swallowing the wrapper's tail.
        count_stmt = sa.text(f"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (\n{q}\n) __dataq_src")  # noqa: S608
        select_stmt = sa.text(
            f"SELECT * FROM (\n{q}\n) __dataq_src LIMIT {int(max_rows) + 1}"  # noqa: S608
        )
    else:
        if not spec.table:
            raise DatasetReadUnsupportedError(
                "SQL comparison side needs a table (or a query)", detail={"spec": "table"}
            )
        if connection.type == "unity_catalog" and not spec.catalog:
            # The UC engine URL deliberately pins no catalog (profiler parity) —
            # an unqualified 2-part name would resolve against the session
            # default catalog and silently read the wrong table.
            raise DatasetReadUnsupportedError(
                "a Unity Catalog comparison side needs a catalog",
                detail={"spec": "catalog"},
            )
        schema = resolve_effective_schema(connection, spec.schema)
        source = _table(schema, spec.table, spec.catalog)
        count_stmt = sa.select(sa.func.count()).select_from(source)
        select_stmt = sa.select(sa.text("*")).select_from(source).limit(max_rows + 1)

    with _open_connection(connection, secret_store) as conn:
        count = int(conn.execute(count_stmt).scalar_one())
        if count > max_rows:
            raise _too_large(count, max_rows, side_hint="dataset")
        # Arrow-backed dtypes for parity with the flat-file/Iceberg readers —
        # cross-side dtype/null-semantics normalization is the #793 engine's
        # contract, but the reader must not hand it numpy-vs-arrow skew of its
        # own making (NULL ints → float64+NaN on one side only).
        df = pd.read_sql(select_stmt, conn, dtype_backend="pyarrow")
    # Belt over the braces: the COUNT is racy (rows can land between preflight
    # and read) and the LIMIT is max_rows+1 exactly so growth is detectable.
    if len(df) > max_rows:
        raise _too_large(len(df), max_rows, side_hint="dataset")
    return df

toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""Read one comparison side (source or target) into a DataFrame (ADR 0015, #792).

The `DatasetReader` seam: given a **datasource** connection and a `DatasetSpec`
(a resolved table triple, a flat-file path, or a read-only SQL projection),
return a bounded pandas DataFrame for the comparison engine (#793). Grown from
plumbing that already exists — the profiler's SQL engine access
(`profile_service`), `flatfile`'s object read, `pyiceberg`'s scan — behind the
same dict-dispatch shape as the profiler's `_PROFILERS`, so service code never
branches on `connection.type`. Orchestration types have no reader (a clean 422,
mirroring `build_check_runner`).

Row-cap discipline (ADR 0015 §3): `max_rows` is enforced **fail-fast** — a
cheap COUNT preflight where the backend offers one (SQL engines, Iceberg scan
metadata) so an oversized table never transfers, plus a post-read length check
everywhere (the preflight is racy by nature; flat files have no preflight at
all). Over-cap raises `DatasetTooLargeError`, **never** a silent truncation —
a truncated diff produces confidently wrong mismatch buckets, which is worse
than no answer.

FastAPI-free like the sibling services: takes ORM `Connection` + `SecretStore`,
returns DataFrames, raises `DataQError` subclasses (the #794 run path maps them
to operational `error` results; the authoring/dry-run paths surface them as
422s).
"""

from __future__ import annotations

from collections.abc import Callable
from dataclasses import dataclass
from typing import Any

import sqlalchemy as sa

from backend.app.core.config import get_settings
from backend.app.core.errors import DataQError
from backend.app.core.logging import get_logger
from backend.app.core.secrets import SecretStore
from backend.app.datasources.flatfile import read_dataframe as read_flatfile_dataframe
from backend.app.datasources.iceberg import (
    IcebergConfig,
    load_iceberg_table,
    read_iceberg_dataframe,
)
from backend.app.db.models import Connection
from backend.app.services.custom_sql import validate_query
from backend.app.services.profile_service import (
    _open_connection,
    _table,
    resolve_effective_schema,
)

log = get_logger(__name__)


class DatasetReadUnsupportedError(DataQError):
    status_code = 422
    code = "comparison_read_unsupported"


class DatasetTooLargeError(DataQError):
    status_code = 422
    code = "comparison_dataset_too_large"


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class DatasetSpec:
    """What to read from one comparison side.

    Exactly one addressing mode is used per datasource: `query` (SQL sources,
    already author-time validated read-only), the `table`/`schema`/`catalog`
    triple (SQL sources; `table` also carries the Iceberg `namespace.table`
    identifier), or `path` (flat-file sources — the caller materializes batch
    targets to a concrete path first, exactly like a run does).
    """

    table: str | None = None
    schema: str | None = None
    catalog: str | None = None
    path: str | None = None
    query: str | None = None


def default_max_rows() -> int:
    """The settings-level comparison row cap (per-check `config.max_rows`
    overrides it — resolved by the caller, ADR 0015 §3)."""
    return get_settings().comparison_max_rows


def _too_large(count: int, max_rows: int, *, side_hint: str) -> DatasetTooLargeError:
    return DatasetTooLargeError(
        f"{side_hint} has {count} rows, over the comparison cap of {max_rows} — "
        "raise config.max_rows deliberately or narrow the dataset (a truncated "
        "diff would be silently wrong, so DataQ refuses instead)",
        detail={"rows": count, "max_rows": max_rows},
    )


def _require_secret(connection: Connection, secret_store: SecretStore) -> str:
    if not connection.secret_ref:
        raise DatasetReadUnsupportedError(
            "connection has no stored credential",
            detail={"connection_id": str(connection.id)},
        )
    return secret_store.get(connection.secret_ref)


# ───────────────────────── SQL (snowflake / unity_catalog) ──────────


def _wrapped_query(spec: DatasetSpec) -> str:
    """The validated read-only projection as a parenthesized FROM source.

    Re-validated here (defence in depth — author-time validation already ran):
    a single read-only SELECT/WITH statement, so interpolating it into the
    COUNT/LIMIT wrappers below cannot smuggle a second statement or a write.
    """
    assert spec.query is not None
    validate_query(spec.query)
    return spec.query.rstrip().rstrip(";")


def _sql_read(
    connection: Connection, spec: DatasetSpec, max_rows: int, secret_store: SecretStore
) -> Any:
    import pandas as pd

    count_stmt: Any
    select_stmt: Any
    if spec.query is not None:
        q = _wrapped_query(spec)
        # Interpolation is safe: `q` passed the read-only single-statement
        # validator (ADR 0019) at author time AND immediately above.
        count_stmt = sa.text(f"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM ({q}) __dataq_src")  # noqa: S608
        select_stmt = sa.text(
            f"SELECT * FROM ({q}) __dataq_src LIMIT {int(max_rows) + 1}"  # noqa: S608
        )
    else:
        schema = resolve_effective_schema(connection, spec.schema)
        source = _table(schema, spec.table or "", spec.catalog)
        count_stmt = sa.select(sa.func.count()).select_from(source)
        select_stmt = sa.select(sa.text("*")).select_from(source).limit(max_rows + 1)

    with _open_connection(connection, secret_store) as conn:
        count = int(conn.execute(count_stmt).scalar_one())
        if count > max_rows:
            raise _too_large(count, max_rows, side_hint="dataset")
        df = pd.read_sql(select_stmt, conn)
    # Belt over the braces: the COUNT is racy (rows can land between preflight
    # and read) and the LIMIT is max_rows+1 exactly so growth is detectable.
    if len(df) > max_rows:
        raise _too_large(len(df), max_rows, side_hint="dataset")
    return df


# ───────────────────────── flat file (adls_gen2 / s3) ───────────────


def _flatfile_read(
    connection: Connection, spec: DatasetSpec, max_rows: int, secret_store: SecretStore
) -> Any:
    path = spec.path or spec.table
    if not path:
        raise DatasetReadUnsupportedError(
            "flat-file comparison side needs a resolved path", detail={"spec": "path"}
        )
    secret = _require_secret(connection, secret_store)
    # No cheap preflight exists for an object read — the whole file downloads,
    # then the cap is enforced before any diffing.
    df = read_flatfile_dataframe(
        conn_type=connection.type, config=dict(connection.config), path=path, secret=secret
    )
    if len(df) > max_rows:
        raise _too_large(len(df), max_rows, side_hint=f"file {path!r}")
    return df


# ───────────────────────── iceberg (native pyiceberg) ───────────────


def _iceberg_read(
    connection: Connection, spec: DatasetSpec, max_rows: int, secret_store: SecretStore
) -> Any:
    if not spec.table:
        raise DatasetReadUnsupportedError(
            "iceberg comparison side needs a namespace.table identifier",
            detail={"spec": "table"},
        )
    cfg = IcebergConfig.model_validate(connection.config)
    # Credential optional, mirroring `build_iceberg_runner` (a local warehouse /
    # vended-credentials REST catalog has none).
    secret = secret_store.get(connection.secret_ref) if connection.secret_ref else None
    table = load_iceberg_table(cfg, secret, spec.table)
    count = int(table.scan().count())  # snapshot metadata — no data files read
    if count > max_rows:
        raise _too_large(count, max_rows, side_hint=f"iceberg table {spec.table!r}")
    df = read_iceberg_dataframe(cfg, secret, spec.table, limit=max_rows + 1, table=table)
    if len(df) > max_rows:
        raise _too_large(len(df), max_rows, side_hint=f"iceberg table {spec.table!r}")
    return df


# ───────────────────────── dispatch ─────────────────────────────────

_Reader = Callable[[Connection, DatasetSpec, int, SecretStore], Any]

_READERS: dict[str, _Reader] = {
    "snowflake": _sql_read,
    "unity_catalog": _sql_read,
    "adls_gen2": _flatfile_read,
    "s3": _flatfile_read,
    "iceberg": _iceberg_read,
}


def read_dataset(
    connection: Connection,
    spec: DatasetSpec,
    *,
    max_rows: int,
    secret_store: SecretStore,
) -> Any:
    """Read one comparison side as a pandas DataFrame, capped at `max_rows`.

    Raises `DatasetReadUnsupportedError` (no reader for the connection type or
    an unusable spec) or `DatasetTooLargeError` (over-cap, fail-fast).
    """
    reader = _READERS.get(connection.type)
    if reader is None:
        raise DatasetReadUnsupportedError(
            f"connection type {connection.type!r} has no dataset reader "
            "(orchestration providers are never comparison sides)",
            detail={"type": connection.type, "supported": sorted(_READERS)},
        )
    if max_rows <= 0:
        raise DatasetReadUnsupportedError(
            "max_rows must be positive", detail={"max_rows": max_rows}
        )
    df = reader(connection, spec, max_rows, secret_store)
    log.info(
        "comparison_side_read",
        connection_id=str(connection.id),
        connection_type=connection.type,
        rows=len(df),
        max_rows=max_rows,
    )
    return df

def test_sql_query_with_trailing_comment_and_semicolons_wraps_safely(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    # Both pass validate_query but broke the old single-line wrapper: a trailing
    # line comment swallowed `) __dataq_src LIMIT …`, and a `; ;` tail survived
    # the old two-step rstrip. The newline-wrapped form neutralizes the comment;
    # the one-pass strip removes the whole tail.
    fake = _patch_sql(monkeypatch, count=1, frame=_frame(1))
    read_dataset(
        _conn("snowflake"),
        DatasetSpec(query="SELECT id FROM T -- latest snapshot"),
        max_rows=10,
        secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )
    assert "-- latest snapshot\n) __dataq_src" in fake.statements[0]

    fake2 = _patch_sql(monkeypatch, count=1, frame=_frame(1))
    read_dataset(
        _conn("snowflake"),
        DatasetSpec(query="SELECT 1; ;"),
        max_rows=10,
        secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )
    assert "SELECT 1;" not in fake2.statements[0]
    assert "SELECT 1\n" in fake2.statements[0]


def test_sql_requires_secret_table_and_uc_catalog(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    _patch_sql(monkeypatch, count=1, frame=_frame(1))
    # Credential-less SQL connection → the same clean 422 as the flat-file path.

        read_dataset(
            _conn("snowflake", secret_ref=None),
            DatasetSpec(table="t", schema="s"),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]

    # Neither query nor table → comparison-branded 422, not a profiler error.

            _conn("snowflake"),

            _conn("unity_catalog"),
            DatasetSpec(table="t", schema="s"),
            max_rows=10,

def test_sql_query_revalidated_read_only_at_read_time(
toolUseResult.originalFile:
"""DatasetReader seam tests (ADR 0015, #792) — no live datasources.

The live I/O seams (`_open_connection`, the flat-file/iceberg read functions)
are monkeypatched exactly like the profiler's tests; what's under test is the
dispatch, the fail-fast row-cap discipline (COUNT preflight + post-read race
guard, never truncation), the read-time read-only re-validation, and the
credential handling.
"""

import re
import uuid
from contextlib import contextmanager
from typing import Any

import pandas as pd
import pytest

from backend.app.db.models import Connection
from backend.app.services import dataset_reader
from backend.app.services.custom_sql import CustomSqlInvalidError
from backend.app.services.dataset_reader import (
    DatasetReadUnsupportedError,
    DatasetSpec,
    DatasetTooLargeError,
    read_dataset,
)


class FakeSecretStore:
    def __init__(self, secret: str | None = "s3cret") -> None:
        self._secret = secret
        self.requested: list[str] = []

    def get(self, ref: str) -> str:
        self.requested.append(ref)
        assert self._secret is not None
        return self._secret


def _conn(conn_type: str, *, secret_ref: str | None = "conn-x", **config: Any) -> Connection:
    return Connection(
        id=uuid.uuid4(),
        name=f"{conn_type}-t",
        type=conn_type,
        env="dev",
        config=config,
        secret_ref=secret_ref,
        created_by=uuid.uuid4(),
    )


def _frame(rows: int) -> pd.DataFrame:
    return pd.DataFrame({"id": range(rows)})


class FakeSqlConnection:
    """Stands in for the SQLAlchemy connection `_open_connection` yields."""

    def __init__(self, count: int) -> None:
        self._count = count
        self.statements: list[str] = []

    def execute(self, stmt: Any) -> Any:
        self.statements.append(str(stmt))
        count = self._count

        class _Result:
            def scalar_one(self) -> int:
                return count

        return _Result()


def _patch_sql(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch, *, count: int, frame: pd.DataFrame
) -> FakeSqlConnection:
    fake = FakeSqlConnection(count)

    @contextmanager
    def _fake_open(connection: Connection, secret_store: Any) -> Any:
        yield fake

    monkeypatch.setattr(dataset_reader, "_open_connection", _fake_open)
    monkeypatch.setattr(pd, "read_sql", lambda stmt, conn: frame)
    return fake


# ───────────────────────── dispatch ─────────────────────────────────


def test_orchestration_type_has_no_reader() -> None:
    with pytest.raises(DatasetReadUnsupportedError, match="orchestration"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("airflow"),
            DatasetSpec(table="t"),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_non_positive_cap_rejected() -> None:
    with pytest.raises(DatasetReadUnsupportedError, match="max_rows"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("snowflake"),
            DatasetSpec(table="t", schema="s"),
            max_rows=0,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_default_max_rows_reads_settings() -> None:
    assert dataset_reader.default_max_rows() == 100_000


# ───────────────────────── SQL path ─────────────────────────────────


def test_sql_table_read_happy_path(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    fake = _patch_sql(monkeypatch, count=3, frame=_frame(3))
    df = read_dataset(
        _conn("snowflake"),
        DatasetSpec(table="ORDERS", schema="RETAIL"),
        max_rows=10,
        secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )
    assert len(df) == 3
    # COUNT preflight ran before the read.
    assert "count" in fake.statements[0].lower()


def test_sql_count_preflight_fails_fast_without_reading(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    def _explode(stmt: Any, conn: Any) -> Any:
        raise AssertionError("read_sql must not run when the preflight is over-cap")

    fake = FakeSqlConnection(count=11)

    @contextmanager
    def _fake_open(connection: Connection, secret_store: Any) -> Any:
        yield fake

    monkeypatch.setattr(dataset_reader, "_open_connection", _fake_open)
    monkeypatch.setattr(pd, "read_sql", _explode)
    with pytest.raises(DatasetTooLargeError, match="11 rows"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("unity_catalog"),
            DatasetSpec(table="t", schema="s", catalog="c"),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_sql_post_read_race_guard(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    # COUNT said 10 (under cap) but rows landed before the read → the LIMIT
    # max_rows+1 read surfaces 11 rows → refuse, never diff a truncated frame.
    _patch_sql(monkeypatch, count=10, frame=_frame(11))
    with pytest.raises(DatasetTooLargeError):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("snowflake"),
            DatasetSpec(table="t", schema="s"),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_sql_query_spec_wraps_and_limits(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    fake = _patch_sql(monkeypatch, count=2, frame=_frame(2))
    df = read_dataset(
        _conn("snowflake"),
        DatasetSpec(query="SELECT id FROM RETAIL.ORDERS;"),
        max_rows=10,
        secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )
    assert len(df) == 2
    assert "SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (SELECT id FROM RETAIL.ORDERS) __dataq_src" in fake.statements[0]


def test_sql_query_revalidated_read_only_at_read_time(
    monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch,
) -> None:
    # Defence in depth: even if a writeful query somehow reached storage, the
    # reader re-validates before interpolating it into the wrappers.
    _patch_sql(monkeypatch, count=1, frame=_frame(1))
    with pytest.raises(CustomSqlInvalidError):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("snowflake"),
            DatasetSpec(query="DELETE FROM ORDERS"),
            max_rows=10,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


# ───────────────────────── flat-file path ───────────────────────────


def test_flatfile_read_and_cap(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    monkeypatch.setattr(dataset_reader, "read_flatfile_dataframe", lambda **kw: _frame(5))
    store = FakeSecretStore()
    df = read_dataset(
        _conn("s3", bucket="b"),
        DatasetSpec(path="orders.csv"),
        max_rows=5,
        secret_store=store,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
    )
    assert len(df) == 5 and store.requested == ["conn-x"]

    monkeypatch.setattr(dataset_reader, "read_flatfile_dataframe", lambda **kw: _frame(6))
    with pytest.raises(DatasetTooLargeError, match=re.escape("orders.csv")):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("adls_gen2"),
            DatasetSpec(path="orders.csv"),
            max_rows=5,
            secret_store=store,  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


def test_flatfile_requires_secret_and_path() -> None:
    with pytest.raises(DatasetReadUnsupportedError, match="credential"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("s3", secret_ref=None),
            DatasetSpec(path="orders.csv"),
            max_rows=5,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )
    with pytest.raises(DatasetReadUnsupportedError, match="path"):
        read_dataset(
            _conn("s3"),
            DatasetSpec(),
            max_rows=5,
            secret_store=FakeSecretStore(),  # type: ignore[arg-type]
        )


# ───────────────────────── iceberg path ─────────────────────────────


class FakeIcebergTable:
    def __init__(self, count: int) -> None:
        self._count = count

    def scan(self) -> Any:
        count = self._count

        class _Scan:
            def count(self) -> int:
                return count

        return _Scan()


_ICEBERG_CONFIG = {
    "catalog_type": "rest",
    "catalog_uri": "http://localhost:8181",
    "warehouse": "wh",
}


def test_iceberg_count_preflight_and_read(monkeypatch: pytest.MonkeyPatch) -> None:
    monkeypatch.setattr(
        dataset_reader, "load_iceberg_table", lambda cfg, secret, ident: FakeIcebergTable(4)
    )
    monkeypatch.setattr(

cd /Users/arijit/Coding/Python/DataQ; git add -A; git commit -m "fixup: code-review findings — UC catalog guard, comment-safe query wrapping, SQL secret pre-check, arrow dtype parity, spec guards

Five agentic-review findings fixed: (1) UC table specs without a catalog
are refused (the engine URL pins none — an unqualified name would read
the session-default catalog's table silently); (2) query wrapping is
newline-delimited + one-pass whitespace/semicolon strip, so a trailing
'-- comment' or '; ;' tail (both validator-legal) can't break the
COUNT/LIMIT wrappers; (3) credential-less SQL connections get the same
clean 422 as flat-file (was _open_connection's bare ValueError → 500);
(4) pd.read_sql uses dtype_backend='pyarrow' for cross-side frame
parity (numpy NULL-int→float64 skew would hand #793 false mismatches);
(5) an empty SQL spec (no table, no query) gets a comparison-branded
422, not the profiler-branded empty-identifier error. Tests extended
(2030 passed, 98.29%).

content.text:
**Slice 2 (#792) is complete and in the merge queue** — PR [#807](https://github.com/TheurgicDuke771/DataQ/pull/807), auto-merge armed with CI running. Summary of the slice:

- `backend/app/services/dataset_reader.py` — the ADR 0015 §3 seam: a `DatasetSpec` (table triple / flat-file path / read-only SQL projection) resolves to a **capped** DataFrame per datasource type, dict-dispatched like the profiler with orchestration types rejected. Fail-fast discipline throughout: `COUNT(*)`/scan-metadata preflight so oversized tables never transfer, `LIMIT max_rows+1` + post-read race guard, typed `DatasetTooLargeError` — never a silent truncation.
- `COMPARISON_MAX_ROWS` setting (100k default, per-check override) documented in the deploy README.
- The review pass caught five real issues, all fixed in-PR: the UC missing-catalog silent-wrong-table trap, validator-legal queries breaking the SQL wrappers (trailing `--` comment / `; ;` tail), a 500 on credential-less SQL connections, numpy-vs-arrow dtype skew that would have fed #793 false mismatches, and a profiler-branded error for empty specs. 2030 tests green at 98.29%.

When the merge notification lands I'll pick up **#793 — the FDC engine port** (record grain, batching latitude, explicit duplicate-key semantics), then the run path (#794) and UI (#795).

attachment.snippet:
1	"""Read one comparison side (source or target) into a DataFrame (ADR 0015, #792).
2	
3	The `DatasetReader` seam: given a **datasource** connection and a `DatasetSpec`
4	(a resolved table triple, a flat-file path, or a read-only SQL projection),
5	return a bounded pandas DataFrame for the comparison engine (#793). Grown from
6	plumbing that already exists — the profiler's SQL engine access
7	(`profile_service`), `flatfile`'s object read, `pyiceberg`'s scan — behind the
8	same dict-dispatch shape as the profiler's `_PROFILERS`, so service code never
9	branches on `connection.type`. Orchestration types have no reader (a clean 422,
10	mirroring `build_check_runner`).
11	
12	Row-cap discipline (ADR 0015 §3): `max_rows` is enforced **fail-fast** — a
13	cheap COUNT preflight where the backend offers one (SQL engines, Iceberg scan
14	metadata) so an oversized table never transfers, plus a post-read length check
15	everywhere (the preflight is racy by nature; flat files have no preflight at
16	all). Over-cap raises `DatasetTooLargeError`, **never** a silent truncation —
17	a truncated diff produces confidently wrong mismatch buckets, which is worse
18	than no answer.
19	
20	FastAPI-free like the sibling services: takes ORM `Connection` + `SecretStore`,
21	returns DataFrames, raises `DataQError` subclasses (the #794 run path maps them
22	to operational `error` results; the authoring/dry-run paths surface them as
23	422s).
24	"""
25	
26	from __future__ import annotations
27	
28	import string
29	from collections.abc import Callable
30	from dataclasses import dataclass
31	from typing import Any
32	
33	import sqlalchemy as sa
34	
35	from backend.app.core.config import get_settings
36	from backend.app.core.errors import DataQError
37	from backend.app.core.logging import get_logger
38	from backend.app.core.secrets import SecretStore
39	from backend.app.datasources.flatfile import read_dataframe as read_flatfile_dataframe
40	from backend.app.datasources.iceberg import (
41	    IcebergConfig,
42	    load_iceberg_table,
43	    read_iceberg_dataframe,
44	)
45	from backend.app.db.models import Connection
46	from backend.app.services.custom_sql import validate_query
47	from backend.app.services.profile_service import (
48	    _open_connection,
49	    _table,
50	    resolve_effective_schema,
51	)
52	
53	log = get_logger(__name__)
54	
55	
56	class DatasetReadUnsupportedError(DataQError):
57	    status_code = 422
58	    code = "comparison_read_unsupported"
59	
60	
61	class DatasetTooLargeError(DataQError):
62	    status_code = 422
63	    code = "comparison_dataset_too_large"
64	
65	
66	@dataclass(frozen=True)
67	class DatasetSpec:
68	    """What to read from one comparison side.
69	
70	    Exactly one addressing mode is used per datasource: `query` (SQL sources,
71	    already author-time validated read-only), the `table`/`schema`/`catalog`
72	    triple (SQL sources; `table` also carries the Iceberg `namespace.table`
73	    identifier), or `path` (flat-file sources — the caller materializes batch
74	    targets to a concrete path first, exactly like a run does).
75	    """
76	
77	    table: str | None = None
78	    schema: str | None = None
79	    catalog: str | None = None
80	    path: str | None = None
81	    query: str | None = None
82	
83	
84	def default_max_rows() -> int:
85	    """The settings-level comparison row cap (per-check `config.max_rows`
86	    overrides it — resolved by the caller, ADR 0015 §3)."""
87	    return get_settings().comparison_max_rows
88	
89	
90	def _too_large(count: int, max_rows: int, *, side_hint: str) -> DatasetTooLargeError:
91	    return DatasetTooLargeError(
92	        f"{side_hint} has {count} rows, over the comparison cap of {max_rows} — "
93	        "raise config.max_rows deliberately or narrow the dataset (a truncated "
94	        "diff would be silently wrong, so DataQ refuses instead)",
95	        detail={"rows": count, "max_rows": max_rows},
96	    )
97	
98	
99	def _require_secret(connection: Connection, secret_store: SecretStore) -> str:
100	    if not connection.secret_ref:
101	        raise DatasetReadUnsupportedError(
102	            "connection has no stored credential",
103	            detail={"connection_id": str(connection.id)},
104	        )
105	    return secret_store.get(connection.secret_ref)
106	
107	
108	# ───────────────────────── SQL (snowflake / unity_catalog) ──────────
109	
110	
111	def _wrapped_query(spec: DatasetSpec) -> str:
112	    """The validated read-only projection as a parenthesized FROM source.
113	
114	    Re-validated here (defence in depth — author-time validation already ran):
115	    a single read-only SELECT/WITH statement, so interpolating it into the
116	    COUNT/LIMIT wrappers below cannot smuggle a second statement or a write.
117	    The whitespace+semicolon tail is stripped in ONE pass (matching
118	    `validate_query`'s own trailing-chars rule — `.rstrip().rstrip(";")` would
119	    leave `SELECT 1; ` from a `; ;` tail and break the wrapper).
120	    """
121	    assert spec.query is not None
122	    validate_query(spec.query)
123	    return spec.query.strip().rstrip(string.whitespace + ";")
124	
125	
126	def _sql_read(
127	    connection: Connection, spec: DatasetSpec, max_rows: int, secret_store: SecretStore
128	) -> Any:
129	    import pandas as pd
130	
131	    if not connection.secret_ref:
132	        # Pre-check so a credential-less connection is the same clean 422 the
133	        # flat-file path gives, not `_open_connection`'s bare ValueError 500.
134	        raise DatasetReadUnsupportedError(
135	            "connection has no stored credential",
136	            detail={"connection_id": str(connection.id)},
137	        )
138	    count_stmt: Any
139	    select_stmt: Any
140	    if spec.query is not None:
141	        q = _wrapped_query(spec)
142	        # Interpolation is safe: `q` passed the read-only single-statement
143	        # validator (ADR 0019) at author time AND immediately above. The
144	        # newline before the closing paren keeps a trailing `-- comment` (legal
145	        # per the validator) from swallowing the wrapper's tail.
146	        count_stmt = sa.text(f"SELECT COUNT(*) FROM (\n{q}\n) __dataq_src")  # noqa: S608  # nosec B608
147	        select_stmt = sa.text(
148	            f"SELECT * FROM (\n{q}\n) __dataq_src LIMIT {int(max_rows) + 1}"  # noqa: S608  # nosec B608
149	        )
150	    else:
151	        if not spec.table:
152	            raise DatasetReadUnsupportedError(
153	                "SQL comparison side needs a table (or a query)", detail={"spec": "table"}
154	            )
155	        if connection.type == "unity_catalog" and not spec.catalog:
156	            # The UC engine URL deliberately pins no catalog (profiler parity) —
157	            # an unqualified 2-part name would resolve against the session
158	            # default catalog and silently read the wrong table.
159	            raise DatasetReadUnsupportedError(
160	                "a Unity Catalog comparison side needs a catalog",
161	                detail={"spec": "catalog"},
162	            )
163	        schema = resolve_effective_schema(connection, spec.schema)
164	        source = _table(schema, spec.table, spec.catalog)
165	        count_stmt = sa.select(sa.func.count()).select_from(source)
166	        select_stmt = sa.select(sa.text("*")).select_from(source).limit(max_rows + 1)
167	
168	    with _open_connection(connection, secret_store) as conn:
169	        count = int(conn.execute(count_stmt).scalar_one())
170	        if count > max_rows:
171	            raise _too_large(count, max_rows, side_hint="dataset")
172	        # Arrow-backed dtypes for parity with the flat-file/Iceberg readers —
173	        # cross-side dtype/null-semantics normalization is the #793 engine's
174	        # contract, but the reader must not hand it numpy-vs-arrow skew of its
175	        # own making (NULL ints → float64+NaN on one side only).
176	        df = pd.read_sql(select_stmt, conn, dtype_backend="pyarrow")
177	    # Belt over the braces: the COUNT is racy (rows can land between preflight
178	    # and read) and the LIMIT is max_rows+1 exactly so growth is detectable.

1	---
2	name: dataq-verify-locally-before-push
3	description: "Run all relevant checks locally (format, lint, typecheck, tests) before committing/pushing — do not use CI as the first feedback loop"
4	metadata:

9	
10	Before committing and pushing changes, run the relevant local checks first. Do not rely on CI to discover format/lint/type/test failures.
11	
12	For frontend changes: `pnpm format:check && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck` (and `pnpm test` once tests exist).
13	For backend changes: `black --check backend/ && ruff check backend/ && mypy backend/app/ && bandit -c pyproject.toml -r backend/app/` (and `pytest` once tests exist). Run inside the conda env (`conda run -n dataq ...`); the base interpreter lacks the deps.
14	
15	**Bandit is a separate CI gate (`Backend — SAST`) that Ruff does NOT cover.** Ruff `# noqa: S105` does not silence Bandit's `B105` — they are independent. PR #74 (4a) passed Ruff locally but failed CI Bandit on `B105 hardcoded_password_string` for a constant whose *name* contained "token". Prefer renaming to avoid the password wordlist (token/secret/pass/pwd/key) over dual `# noqa: S105` + `# nosec B105` suppression.
16	
17	**Why:** Established after a CI-spam incident during PR #25 (PR1 tooling lock) where successive pushes each broke CI in a new way (Prettier on generated lockfile, ESLint v9 filename, TS6306/6310 on tsconfig.node.json). User said: "failed again, please check locally if possible before commit (we are spamming it now)." Re-confirmed after PR1 close-out: "going forward lets check in local when possible then."
18	
19	**How to apply:** Treat local green as the gate, push as the publish step. When a hook fails, fix the underlying issue — do not `--no-verify`. If a local check cannot reasonably be run (e.g., requires deployed Azure infra), say so explicitly rather than skipping silently.
20	
21	Related: [[dataq-working-agreements]], [[dataq-review-before-merge]]

file.content:
---
name: dataq-verify-locally-before-push
description: "Run all relevant checks locally (format, lint, typecheck, tests) before committing/pushing — do not use CI as the first feedback loop"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: feedback
  originSessionId: 20430269-c3d6-46f0-a275-830e2c7009f4
---

Before committing and pushing changes, run the relevant local checks first. Do not rely on CI to discover format/lint/type/test failures.

For frontend changes: `pnpm format:check && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck` (and `pnpm test` once tests exist).
For backend changes: `black --check backend/ && ruff check backend/ && mypy backend/app/ && bandit -c pyproject.toml -r backend/app/` (and `pytest` once tests exist). Run inside the conda env (`conda run -n dataq ...`); the base interpreter lacks the deps.

**Bandit is a separate CI gate (`Backend — SAST`) that Ruff does NOT cover.** Ruff `# noqa: S105` does not silence Bandit's `B105` — they are independent. PR #74 (4a) passed Ruff locally but failed CI Bandit on `B105 hardcoded_password_string` for a constant whose *name* contained "token". Prefer renaming to avoid the password wordlist (token/secret/pass/pwd/key) over dual `# noqa: S105` + `# nosec B105` suppression.

**Why:** Established after a CI-spam incident during PR #25 (PR1 tooling lock) where successive pushes each broke CI in a new way (Prettier on generated lockfile, ESLint v9 filename, TS6306/6310 on tsconfig.node.json). User said: "failed again, please check locally if possible before commit (we are spamming it now)." Re-confirmed after PR1 close-out: "going forward lets check in local when possible then."

**How to apply:** Treat local green as the gate, push as the publish step. When a hook fails, fix the underlying issue — do not `--no-verify`. If a local check cannot reasonably be run (e.g., requires deployed Azure infra), say so explicitly rather than skipping silently.

Related: [[dataq-working-agreements]], [[dataq-review-before-merge]]

**Bandit is a separate CI gate (`Backend — SAST`) that Ruff does NOT cover.** Ruff `# noqa: S105` does not silence Bandit's `B105` — they are independent. PR #74 (4a) passed Ruff locally but failed CI Bandit on `B105 hardcoded_password_string` for a constant whose *name* contained "token". Prefer renaming to avoid the password wordlist (token/secret/pass/pwd/key) over dual `# noqa: S105` + `# nosec B105` suppression.

**Local bandit clean ≠ CI bandit clean (version divergence, PR #807 2026-07-12):** local bandit didn't flag f-string SQL inside `sa.text(...)` (B608) and even warned my `# nosec B608` was unused — the CI-pinned bandit DID flag it and failed the gate. When intentionally writing something bandit-adjacent (interpolated SQL, secret-wordlist names), keep BOTH `# noqa: S6xx` and `# nosec B6xx` with a justification comment even if local bandit calls the nosec unused; the "unused nosec" is a warning, the missing one is a CI failure.

---
name: dataq-verify-locally-before-push
description: "Run all relevant checks locally (format, lint, typecheck, tests) before committing/pushing — do not use CI as the first feedback loop"
metadata: 
  node_type: memory
  type: feedback
  originSessionId: 20430269-c3d6-46f0-a275-830e2c7009f4
---

Before committing and pushing changes, run the relevant local checks first. Do not rely on CI to discover format/lint/type/test failures.

For frontend changes: `pnpm format:check && pnpm lint && pnpm typecheck` (and `pnpm test` once tests exist).
For backend changes: `black --check backend/ && ruff check backend/ && mypy backend/app/ && bandit -c pyproject.toml -r backend/app/` (and `pytest` once tests exist). Run inside the conda env (`conda run -n dataq ...`); the base interpreter lacks the deps.

**Bandit is a separate CI gate (`Backend — SAST`) that Ruff does NOT cover.** Ruff `# noqa: S105` does not silence Bandit's `B105` — they are independent. PR #74 (4a) passed Ruff locally but failed CI Bandit on `B105 hardcoded_password_string` for a constant whose *name* contained "token". Prefer renaming to avoid the password wordlist (token/secret/pass/pwd/key) over dual `# noqa: S105` + `# nosec B105` suppression.

**Why:** Established after a CI-spam incident during PR #25 (PR1 tooling lock) where successive pushes each broke CI in a new way (Prettier on generated lockfile, ESLint v9 filename, TS6306/6310 on tsconfig.node.json). User said: "failed again, please check locally if possible before commit (we are spamming it now)." Re-confirmed after PR1 close-out: "going forward lets check in local when possible then."

**How to apply:** Treat local green as the gate, push as the publish step. When a hook fails, fix the underlying issue — do not `--no-verify`. If a local check cannot reasonably be run (e.g., requires deployed Azure infra), say so explicitly rather than skipping silently.

Related: [[dataq-working-agreements]], [[dataq-review-before-merge]]

+**Local bandit clean ≠ CI bandit clean (version divergence, PR #807 2026-07-12):** local bandit didn't flag f-string SQL inside `sa.text(...)` (B608) and even warned my `# nosec B608` was unused — the CI-pinned bandit DID flag it and failed the gate. When intentionally writing something bandit-adjacent (interpolated SQL, secret-wordlist names), keep BOTH `# noqa: S6xx` and `# nosec B6xx` with a justification comment even if local bandit calls the nosec unused; the "unused nosec" is a warning, the missing one is a CI failure.

"""Record-level comparator: diff two DataFrames row-by-row using an outer merge."""

import time
import traceback

from .comparison_reports import (
    generate_comparison_summary_report,
    generate_record_comparison_report,
)
from .comparison_utils import (
    ColumnsLengthMismatch,
    ColumnsNamesMismatch,
    check_columns_length,
    check_columns_names,
    handling_datatypes,
)
from .record_response import RecordResponse


def meaningful_names(name):
    """Translate pandas ``_merge`` indicator values into human-readable labels.

    Args:
        name: One of ``"both"``, ``"left_only"``, or ``"right_only"``.

    Returns:
        str: ``"Matched"`` for ``"both"``, ``"Source"`` for ``"left_only"``,
        ``"Target"`` for anything else.
    """
    if name == "both":
        return "Matched"
    if name == "left_only":
        return "Source"
    return "Target"


def dataframes_record_based_comparison(tc_id, src_df, tgt_df, report_type, report_file_type, is_test=False):
    """Compare two DataFrames row-by-row and produce output reports.

    Uses pandas' outer merge with an indicator column to bucket rows into
    matched, source-only, and target-only sets. Target columns are force-
    renamed to match source column names before the merge.

    Args:
        tc_id (str): Testcase ID, used to namespace the results directory.
        src_df (pd.DataFrame): Source DataFrame.
        tgt_df (pd.DataFrame): Target DataFrame.
        report_type (str): Which comparison report to create. Allowed values:

            * ``summary`` — summary of matched and mismatched records.
            * ``detailed_mismatched`` — mismatches + rows only in source / target.
            * ``detailed_matched`` — matched records only.
        report_file_type (str): ``csv`` or ``excel``.
        is_test (bool, optional): Internal test/debug flag; short-circuits
            before writing reports. Defaults to ``False``.

    Raises:
        ColumnsLengthMismatch: When source and target have different column counts.
        ColumnsNamesMismatch: When source and target have different column names.

    Returns:
        tuple[str, dict]: ``(message, details)`` where ``message`` is
        ``"success"``, a human-readable error string, or an internal-error
        string, and ``details`` is a dict of per-bucket counts or an error
        payload.
    """
    print("*" * 50, "Record comparison started", "*" * 50)
    print("src_df", src_df)
    print("---")
    print("tgt_df", tgt_df)
    print(tgt_df.columns)
    print(src_df.columns)

    start_time = time.time()

    record_response = RecordResponse()
    message = ""
    try:
        if not check_columns_length(src_df, tgt_df):
            raise ColumnsLengthMismatch

        # Renaming target columns to maintain consistency (only safe after length check)
        tgt_df.columns = src_df.columns

        if not check_columns_names(src_df, tgt_df):
            raise ColumnsNamesMismatch
        # Handling different datatypes
        src_df, tgt_df = handling_datatypes(src_df, tgt_df)

        # Number of rows in src and target
        nrow_src = src_df.shape[0]
        nrow_tgt = tgt_df.shape[0]

        # Making comparison dataframe
        comparison_df = src_df.merge(tgt_df, indicator=True, how="outer")

        # Meaningful naming
        comparison_df["_merge"] = comparison_df["_merge"].apply(meaningful_names)
        comparison_df.rename(columns={"_merge": "Mismatches"}, inplace=True)

        # Making required dataframes
        diff_df = comparison_df[comparison_df["Mismatches"] != "Matched"]
        rows_similar = comparison_df[comparison_df["Mismatches"] == "Matched"]
        rows_s_minus_t = comparison_df[comparison_df["Mismatches"] == "Source"]
        rows_t_minus_s = comparison_df[comparison_df["Mismatches"] == "Target"]

        # Number of rows
        no_of_rows_similar = rows_similar.shape[0]
        no_of_rows_s_t = rows_s_minus_t.shape[0]
        no_of_rows_t_s = rows_t_minus_s.shape[0]
        mismatched_records = diff_df.shape[0]

        # Attribute names
        attribute_names = src_df.columns
        print("Matched file is :", rows_similar.head())
        print("Mismatched file is:", diff_df.head())

        print("*" * 30, "Record Comparison Report", "*" * 30)
        print(f"Total number of records present in source : {nrow_src}")
        print(f"Total number of records present in target : {nrow_tgt}")

def dataframes_column_based_comparison(tc_id, src, tgt, primary_key, report_type, report_file_type, is_test=False):
    """Compare two DataFrames column-by-column joined on one or more primary keys.

    Args:
        tc_id (str): Testcase ID, used to namespace the results directory.
        src (pd.DataFrame): Source DataFrame.
        tgt (pd.DataFrame): Target DataFrame.
        primary_key (str): One or more column names (comma-separated) that
            uniquely identify rows across source and target.
        report_type (str): Which comparison report to create. Allowed values:

            * ``summary`` — summary of matched and mismatched records.
            * ``detailed_mismatched`` — mismatches + rows only in source / target.
            * ``detailed_matched`` — matched records only.
        report_file_type (str): Output file format. Allowed values:

            * ``csv`` — comma-separated text (``.csv``).
            * ``excel`` — spreadsheet (``.xlsx``).
        is_test (bool, optional): Internal test/debug flag; short-circuits
            before writing reports. Defaults to ``False``.

    Raises:
        ColumnsLengthMismatch: When source and target have different column counts.
        ColumnsNamesMismatch: When source and target have different column names.

    Returns:
        tuple[str, dict]: ``(message, details)`` where ``message`` is one of
        ``"success"``, a human-readable error string, or ``"failure"``, and
        ``details`` is a dict of per-row counts or an error payload.
    """
    print("*" * 50, "Column comparison started", "*" * 50)
    print("src_df", src.head())
    print("tgt_df", tgt.head())

    start_time = time.time()

    response = ColumnResponse()
    mismatch_data = {}
    additional_in_source = {}
    additional_in_target = {}

    column_wise_mismatch_dict = {}
    column_wise_additional_in_source_dict = {}
    column_wise_additional_in_target_dict = {}
    mismatched_count = 0
    matched_count = 0
    primary_key = [k.strip() for k in primary_key.split(",")]

    try:
        if not check_columns_length(src, tgt):
            raise ColumnsLengthMismatch

        # Renaming target columns to maintain consistency (only safe after length check)
        tgt.columns = src.columns

        if not check_columns_names(src, tgt):
            raise ColumnsNamesMismatch

src, tgt = handling_datatypes(src, tgt)

        matched_df, rec_count = match(src, tgt)

            df, additional_in_source_df, additional_in_target_df = mismatch(src, tgt, primary_key, col)

                additional_in_target[col] = additional_in_target_df

                column_wise_mismatch_dict[col] = len(mismatch_data[col])
                column_wise_additional_in_source_dict[col] = len(additional_in_source[col])
                column_wise_additional_in_target_dict[col] = len(additional_in_target[col])

        column_wise_mismatch_dict = drop_empty_dfs(column_wise_mismatch_dict)

        print(f"Total number of records present in target : {tgt.shape[0]}")
        print(f"Records present in both source and target : {matched_count}")
        print(f"Total number of mismatched values present : {mismatched_count}")
        print("column_wise_mismatch : ", json.dumps(column_wise_mismatch_dict, indent=4))
        end_time = time.time()

            matched_csv,
            mismatched_excel,
            additional_in_source_file,
            additional_in_target_file,
        ) = generate_column_comparison_report(
            tc_id,
            mismatch_data,
            report_type,
            matched_df,
            additional_in_source,
            additional_in_target,
            report_file_type,

        generate_comparison_summary_report(tc_id, "Column_Comparison", response.get_json_representaion())

        print("Completed comparison!")
        print(f"Time Elapsed : {round(end_time - start_time, 2)}")
        print("---------@@@@@@@@---------")

        message = "success"
        details = {
            "Comparison Type": "Column Level",
            "Primary Key": primary_key,
            "Total number of records present in source": src.shape[0],
            "Total number of records present in target": tgt.shape[0],
            "Records present in both source and target": matched_count,
            "Total number of mismatched values present": mismatched_count,
        }

    except ColumnsLengthMismatch:
        message = "Length of Source and target columns is different"
        details = {"Error Message": message}

    except ColumnsNamesMismatch:
        message = "Names of Source and target columns are different"
        details = {"Error Message": message}

    except Exception as e:
        print("Exception has occurred in Column comparison function : ", str(e))
        print(traceback.format_exc())
        message = "failure"
        details = {"Error Message": str(e)}

    return message, details

content.content:
def match(source, target):
    """Return the inner-join of two DataFrames and its row count.

    De-duplicates both inputs first (keeping the first occurrence).

    Args:
        source (pd.DataFrame): Source DataFrame (mutated: duplicates dropped in place).
        target (pd.DataFrame): Target DataFrame (mutated: duplicates dropped in place).

    Returns:
        tuple[pd.DataFrame, int]: ``(merged_df, row_count)``.
    """
    source.drop_duplicates(keep="first", inplace=True)
    target.drop_duplicates(keep="first", inplace=True)
    res = pd.merge(source, target, how="inner")
    return (res, res.shape[0])


def mismatch(source, target, primary_key, col):
    """Diff a single column between source and target, keyed by ``primary_key``.

    Performs an outer merge on ``primary_key`` and splits rows into three
    buckets based on the ``col`` value: actual mismatches (present in both
    but different), rows only in source, and rows only in target.

    Args:
        source (pd.DataFrame): Source DataFrame.
        target (pd.DataFrame): Target DataFrame.
        primary_key (list[str]): Primary-key column names.
        col (str): Name of the column being compared.

    Returns:
        tuple[pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame, pd.DataFrame]: ``(mismatch_df,
        additional_in_source, additional_in_target)``.
    """
    mer = source.merge(target, on=primary_key, how="outer", suffixes=("_src", "_tgt"))
    mismatch = mer[mer[str(col) + "_src"] != mer[str(col) + "_tgt"]]
    display_col = [*primary_key, str(col) + "_src", str(col) + "_tgt"]

    try:
        actual_mismatch = mismatch[display_col][
            mismatch[display_col][col + "_src"].notna() & mismatch[display_col][col + "_tgt"].notna()
        ]
        either_in_source_target = mismatch[display_col][
            mismatch[display_col][col + "_src"].isna() | mismatch[display_col][col + "_tgt"].isna()
        ]
        additional_in_source = either_in_source_target.iloc[:, [0, 1]].dropna()
        additional_in_target = either_in_source_target.iloc[:, [0, 2]].dropna()

    except Exception as E:
        print("Exception has occured while matching:", str(E))

    return actual_mismatch, additional_in_source, additional_in_target
